1	import path from 'node:path'

3	import { BuildOptions } from './build'

5	type AdapterEntry = {
6	  entry: string
7	  outfile: string
8	}
9	
10	export function createAdapterConfig(entries: AdapterEntry[]): BuildOptions[] {
11	  return entries.flatMap(({ entry, outfile }) => {
12	    const baseName = path.basename(outfile)
13	
14	    return [
15	      {
16	        name: `${baseName}-cjs`,
17	        format: 'cjs' as const,
18	        bundle: true,
19	        entryPoints: [entry],
20	        outfile,
21	        outExtension: { '.js': '.js' },
22	        emitTypes: true,
23	      },
24	      {
25	        name: `${baseName}-esm`,
26	        format: 'esm' as const,
27	        bundle: true,
28	        entryPoints: [entry],
29	        outfile,
30	        outExtension: { '.js': '.mjs' },
31	        emitTypes: true,
32	      },
33	    ]
34	  })
35	}
36	
37	export const adapterConfig = createAdapterConfig([{ entry: 'src/index.ts', outfile: 'dist/index' }])
38	
39	export const unbundledConfig: BuildOptions[] = [
40	  {
41	    name: 'cjs',
42	    format: 'cjs',
43	    entryPoints: ['src/**/*.ts'],
44	    outdir: 'dist',
45	    outExtension: { '.js': '.js' },

52	    outdir: 'dist',

1	import { ChokidarOptions, watch as createWatcher } from 'chokidar'
2	import * as esbuild from 'esbuild'
3	import { BuildContext } from 'esbuild'
4	import { writeFileSync } from 'fs'
5	import glob from 'globby'
6	import path from 'path'
7	
8	import { debounce } from '../blaze/debounce'
9	import { flatten } from '../blaze/flatten'
10	import { handle } from '../blaze/handle'
11	import { map } from '../blaze/map'
12	import { omit } from '../blaze/omit'
13	import { pipe } from '../blaze/pipe'
14	import { transduce } from '../blaze/transduce'
15	import { fixImportsPlugin } from './plugins/fixImportsPlugin'
16	import { onErrorPlugin } from './plugins/onErrorPlugin'
17	import { resolvePathsPlugin } from './plugins/resolvePathsPlugin'
18	import { tscPlugin } from './plugins/tscPlugin'
19	
20	export type BuildResult = esbuild.BuildResult
21	export type BuildOptions = esbuild.BuildOptions & {
22	  name?: string
23	  emitTypes?: boolean
24	  emitMetafile?: boolean
25	  outbase?: never // we don't support this
26	}
27	
28	const DEFAULT_BUILD_OPTIONS = {
29	  platform: 'node',
30	  target: 'ES2022',
31	  logLevel: 'error',
32	  tsconfig: 'tsconfig.build.json',
33	  metafile: true,
34	} as const
35	
36	/**
37	 * Apply defaults to the original build options
38	 * @param options the original build options
39	 */
40	const applyDefaults = (options: BuildOptions): BuildOptions => ({
41	  ...DEFAULT_BUILD_OPTIONS,
42	  format: 'cjs',
43	  outExtension: { '.js': '.js' },
44	  resolveExtensions: ['.ts', '.js', '.node'],
45	  entryPoints: glob.sync('./src/**/*.{j,t}s', {
46	    ignore: ['./src/__tests__/**/*'],
47	  }),
48	  mainFields: ['module', 'main'],
49	  ...options,
50	  // outfile has precedence over outdir, hence these ternaries
51	  outfile: options.outfile ? getOutFile(options) : undefined,
52	  outdir: options.outfile ? undefined : getOutDir(options),
53	  plugins: [
54	    ...(options.plugins ?? []),
55	    resolvePathsPlugin,
56	    fixImportsPlugin,
57	    tscPlugin(options.emitTypes),
58	    onErrorPlugin,
59	  ],
60	  external: [...(options.external ?? []), ...getProjectExternals(options)],
61	})
62	
63	/**
64	 * Create two deferred builds for esm and cjs. The one follows the other:
65	 * - 1. The code gets compiled to an optimized tree-shaken esm output
66	 * - 2. We take that output and compile it to an optimized cjs output
67	 * @param options the original build options
68	 * @returns if options = [a, b], we get [a-esm, a-cjs, b-esm, b-cjs]
69	 */
70	function createBuildOptions(options: BuildOptions[]) {
71	  return flatten(
72	    map(options, (options) => [
73	      // we defer it so that we don't trigger glob immediately
74	      () => applyDefaults(options),
75	      // ... here can go more steps
76	    ]),
77	  )
78	}
79	
80	/**
81	 * We only want to trigger the glob search once we are ready, and that is when
82	 * the previous build has finished. We get the build options from the deferred.
83	 */
84	function computeOptions(options: () => BuildOptions) {
85	  return options()
86	}
87	
88	/**
89	 * Extensions are not automatically by esbuild set for `options.outfile`. We
90	 * look at the set `options.outExtension` and we add that to `options.outfile`.
91	 */
92	function addExtensionFormat(options: BuildOptions) {
93	  if (options.outfile && options.outExtension) {
94	    const ext = options.outExtension['.js']
95	
96	    options.outfile = `${options.outfile}${ext}`
97	  }
98	
99	  return options
100	}
101	
102	/**
103	 * If we don't have `options.outfile`, we default `options.outdir`
104	 */
105	function addDefaultOutDir(options: BuildOptions) {
106	  if (options.outfile === undefined) {
107	    options.outdir = getOutDir(options)
108	  }
109	
110	  return options
111	}
112	
113	/**
114	 * Execute esbuild with all the configurations we pass
115	 */
116	async function executeEsBuild(options: BuildOptions) {
117	  if (process.env.WATCH === 'true') {
118	    const context = await esbuild.context(omit(options, ['name', 'emitTypes', 'emitMetafile']) as any)
119	
120	    watch(context, options)
121	  }
122	
123	  const build = await esbuild.build(omit(options, ['name', 'emitTypes', 'emitMetafile']) as any)
124	  const outdir = options.outdir ?? (options.outfile ? path.dirname(options.outfile) : undefined)
125	
126	  if (build.metafile && options.emitMetafile) {
127	    const metafilePath = `${outdir}/${options.name}.meta.json`
128	    writeFileSync(metafilePath, JSON.stringify(build.metafile))
129	  }
130	
131	  return [options, build] as const
132	}
133	
134	/**
135	 * Execution pipeline that applies a set of actions
136	 * @param options
137	 */
138	export async function build(options: BuildOptions[]) {
139	  return transduce.async(
140	    createBuildOptions(options),
141	    pipe.async(computeOptions, logStartBuild, addExtensionFormat, addDefaultOutDir, executeEsBuild),
142	  )
143	}
144	
145	/**
146	 * Prints a message every time a new bundle is built
147	 */
148	function logStartBuild(options: BuildOptions): BuildOptions {
149	  console.log(`Building ${options.name} as ${options.format ?? 'cjs'}...`)
150	  return options
151	}
152	
153	/**
154	 * Executes the build and rebuilds what is necessary
155	 * @param builds
156	 */
157	const watch = (context: BuildContext, options: BuildOptions) => {
158	  if (process.env.WATCH !== 'true') return context
159	
160	  // common chokidar options for the watchers
161	  const config = {
162	    ignoreInitial: true,
163	    ignored: [/$src\/__tests__\//, 'package.json'],
164	  } satisfies ChokidarOptions
165	
166	  // prepare the incremental builds watcher
167	  const changeWatcher = createWatcher(['./src'], config)
168	
169	  // triggers quick rebuild on file change
170	  const fastRebuild = debounce(async () => {
171	    const timeBefore = Date.now()
172	
173	    // we handle possible rebuild exceptions
174	    const rebuildResult = await handle.async(() => {
175	      return context.rebuild()
176	    })
177	
178	    // Handle unexpected internal errors
179	    if (rebuildResult instanceof Error) {
180	      console.error(rebuildResult)
181	      // Handle build errors (e.g., syntax errors)
182	    } else if (rebuildResult.errors.length > 0) {
183	      // Log the detailed error object from esbuild for better debugging.
184	      console.error(rebuildResult.errors)
185	    }
186	
187	    console.log(`${Date.now() - timeBefore}ms [${options.name ?? ''}]`)
188	  }, 10)
189	
190	  changeWatcher.on('change', fastRebuild)
191	
192	  return undefined
193	}
194	
195	// Utils ::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::
196	
197	// get a default directory if needed (no outfile)
198	function getOutDir(options: BuildOptions) {
199	  if (options.outfile !== undefined) {
200	    return path.dirname(options.outfile)
201	  }
202	
203	  return options.outdir ?? 'dist'
204	}
205	
206	// get the output file from an original path
207	function getOutFile(options: BuildOptions) {
208	  if (options.outfile !== undefined) {
209	    const dirname = getOutDir(options)
210	    const filename = path.basename(options.outfile)
211	
212	    return `${dirname}/${filename}`
213	  }
214	
215	  return undefined
216	}
217	
218	// get the current project externals this helps to mark dependencies as external
219	// by having convention in the package.json (dev = bundled, non-dev = external)
220	function getProjectExternals(options: BuildOptions) {
221	  const pkg = require(`${process.cwd()}/package.json`)
222	  const peerDeps = Object.keys(pkg.peerDependencies ?? {})
223	  const regDeps = Object.keys(pkg.dependencies ?? {})
224	
225	  // when bundling, only the devDeps will be bundled
226	  if (!process.env.IGNORE_EXTERNALS && options.bundle === true) {
227	    return [...new Set([...peerDeps, ...regDeps])]
228	  }
229	
230	  // otherwise, all the dependencies will be bundled
231	  return []
232	}

11	type PrismaClientJsGeneratorOptions = {
12	  shouldResolvePrismaClient?: boolean
13	  shouldInstallMissingPackages?: boolean
14	  runtimePath?: string
15	}
16	
17	// const MISSING_CUSTOM_OUTPUT_PATH_WARNING = `\
18	// ${yellow('Warning:')} You did not specify an output path for your \`generator\` in schema.prisma. \
19	// This behavior is deprecated and will no longer be supported in Prisma 7.0.0. To learn more \
20	// visit https://pris.ly/cli/output-path`
21	
22	export class PrismaClientJsGenerator implements Generator {
23	  readonly name = BuiltInProvider.PrismaClientJs
24	
25	  #shouldResolvePrismaClient: boolean
26	  #runtimePath?: string
27	  #cachedPrismaClientPath: string | undefined
28	
29	  constructor({ shouldResolvePrismaClient = true, runtimePath }: PrismaClientJsGeneratorOptions = {}) {
30	    this.#shouldResolvePrismaClient = shouldResolvePrismaClient
31	    this.#runtimePath = runtimePath
32	  }
33	
34	  async getManifest(config: GeneratorConfig): Promise<GeneratorManifest> {
35	    // TODO: warning disabled for now until we fixed issues around custom output paths - see ORM-976
36	    // if (!config.output) {
37	    //   console.warn(MISSING_CUSTOM_OUTPUT_PATH_WARNING)
38	    // }
39	
40	    // If `this.#shouldResolvePrismaClient` is true, which is normally the case,
41	    // we find the default output path by resolving the path to the
42	    // `@prisma/client` package. While we resolve the absolute path to the
43	    // package itself, the generator will rewrite it to replace
44	    // `.../@prisma/client` with `.../.prisma/client`, as long as
45	    // `config.isCustomOutput` is false.
46	    //
47	    // If a custom output path is provided, then the default output path doesn't
48	    // matter and we could use a static value here, but that unfortunately
49	    // wouldn't allow us to avoid resolving the client directory and would only
50	    // delay that until the `generate` method call because we copy the runtime
51	    // files if a custom output path is used, and we also copy the WASM modules
52	    // regardles of the output path. Not copying the runtime files is a breaking
53	    // change, so we can only completely get rid of this behaviour in the new
54	    // generator.
55	    //
56	    // If `this.#shouldResolvePrismaClient` is false, which is the case when
57	    // using the JSON-RPC compatibility adapter for Prisma Studio, we should use
58	    // a static value here.
59	    const defaultOutput = this.#shouldResolvePrismaClient ? await this.#getPrismaClientPath(config) : '.prisma/client'
60	
61	    return {
62	      defaultOutput,
63	      prettyName: 'Prisma Client',
64	      version: clientVersion,
65	      requiresEngines: [],
66	      requiresEngineVersion: enginesVersion,
67	    }
68	  }
69	
70	  async generate(options: GeneratorOptions): Promise<void> {
71	    const outputDir = parseEnvValue(options.generator.output!)
72	
73	    await generateClient({
74	      datamodel: options.datamodel,
75	      schemaPath: options.schemaPath,
76	      binaryPaths: options.binaryPaths!,
77	      datasources: options.datasources,
78	      outputDir,
79	      copyRuntime: Boolean(options.generator.config.copyRuntime),
80	      copyRuntimeSourceMaps: Boolean(process.env.PRISMA_COPY_RUNTIME_SOURCEMAPS),
81	      runtimeSourcePath: await this.#getRuntimePath(options.generator),
82	      dmmf: options.dmmf,
83	      generator: options.generator,
84	      engineVersion: options.version,
85	      clientVersion,
86	      activeProvider: options.datasources[0]?.activeProvider,
87	      typedSql: options.typedSql,
88	      compilerBuild: parseCompilerBuildFromUnknown(options.generator.config.compilerBuild),
89	    })
90	  }
91	
92	  async #getPrismaClientPath(config: GeneratorConfig): Promise<string> {
93	    if (this.#cachedPrismaClientPath) {
94	      return this.#cachedPrismaClientPath
95	    }
96	
97	    this.#cachedPrismaClientPath = await resolvePrismaClient(path.dirname(config.sourceFilePath))
98	    return this.#cachedPrismaClientPath
99	  }
100	
101	  async #getRuntimePath(config: GeneratorConfig): Promise<string> {
102	    if (this.#runtimePath) {
103	      return this.#runtimePath
104	    }
105	
106	    this.#runtimePath = path.join(await this.#getPrismaClientPath(config), 'runtime')
107	    return this.#runtimePath
108	  }
109	}
110	
111	function parseCompilerBuildFromUnknown(value: unknown): 'fast' | 'small' {
112	  if (value === undefined) {
113	    return 'fast'
114	  }
115	  if (value === 'small' || value === 'fast') {
116	    return value
117	  }
118	  throw new Error(`Invalid compiler build: ${JSON.stringify(value)}, expected one of: "fast", "small"`)
119	}
120

2	import { Generator, GeneratorConfig, GeneratorManifest, GeneratorOptions } from '@prisma/generator'
3	import { parseEnvValue } from '@prisma/internals'
4	import { getTsconfig } from 'get-tsconfig'
5	import { bold, dim, green } from 'kleur/colors'
6	
7	import { version as clientVersion } from '../package.json'
8	import { inferImportFileExtension, parseGeneratedFileExtension, parseImportFileExtension } from './file-extensions'
9	import { generateClient } from './generateClient'
10	import { inferModuleFormat, parseModuleFormatFromUnknown } from './module-format'
11	import { parseRuntimeTargetFromUnknown, RuntimeTargetInternal } from './runtime-targets'
12	
13	const missingOutputErrorMessage = `An output path is required for the \`prisma-client\` generator. Please provide an output path in your schema file:
14	
15	${dim(`generator client {
16	  provider = "prisma-client"`)}
17	${green('  output   = "../src/generated"')}
18	${dim('}')}
19	
20	${bold('Note:')} the output path is relative to the schema directory.
21	`
22	
23	function getOutputPath(config: GeneratorConfig): string {
24	  if (!config.output) {
25	    throw new Error(missingOutputErrorMessage)
26	  }
27	  return parseEnvValue(config.output)
28	}
29	
30	export class PrismaClientTsGenerator implements Generator {
31	  readonly name = 'prisma-client-ts'
32	
33	  getManifest(config: GeneratorConfig): Promise<GeneratorManifest> {
34	    return Promise.resolve({
35	      defaultOutput: getOutputPath(config),
36	      prettyName: 'Prisma Client',
37	      version: clientVersion,
38	      requiresEngines: [],
39	      requiresEngineVersion: enginesVersion,
40	    })
41	  }
42	
43	  async generate(options: GeneratorOptions): Promise<void> {
44	    const { config } = options.generator
45	    const outputDir = getOutputPath(options.generator)
46	    const tsconfig = getTsconfig(outputDir)?.config
47	
48	    const target = config.runtime !== undefined ? parseRuntimeTargetFromUnknown(config.runtime) : 'nodejs'
49	
50	    const generatedFileExtension =
51	      config.generatedFileExtension !== undefined ? parseGeneratedFileExtension(config.generatedFileExtension) : 'ts'
52	
53	    const importFileExtension =
54	      config.importFileExtension !== undefined
55	        ? parseImportFileExtension(config.importFileExtension)
56	        : inferImportFileExtension({
57	            tsconfig,
58	            generatedFileExtension,
59	            target,
60	          })
61	
62	    const moduleFormat =
63	      config.moduleFormat !== undefined
64	        ? parseModuleFormatFromUnknown(config.moduleFormat)
65	        : inferModuleFormat({
66	            tsconfig,
67	            generatedFileExtension,
68	            importFileExtension,
69	          })
70	
71	    await generateClient({
72	      datamodel: options.datamodel,
73	      schemaPath: options.schemaPath,
74	      binaryPaths: options.binaryPaths!,
75	      datasources: options.datasources,
76	      outputDir,
77	      runtimeBase: '@prisma/client/runtime',
78	      dmmf: options.dmmf,
79	      generator: options.generator,
80	      engineVersion: options.version,
81	      clientVersion,
82	      activeProvider: options.datasources[0]?.activeProvider,
83	      typedSql: options.typedSql,
84	      target,
85	      generatedFileExtension,
86	      importFileExtension,
87	      moduleFormat,
88	      tsNoCheckPreamble: true, // Set to false only during internal tests
89	      compilerBuild: parseCompilerBuildFromUnknown(options.generator.config.compilerBuild, target),
90	    })
91	  }
92	}
93	
94	function parseCompilerBuildFromUnknown(value: unknown, target: RuntimeTargetInternal): 'fast' | 'small' {
95	  if (value === undefined) {
96	    // using the 'small' build for 'vercel-edge' target to fit within their free tier limits
97	    return target === 'vercel-edge' ? 'small' : 'fast'
98	  }
99	  if (value === 'small' || value === 'fast') {
100	    return value
101	  }
102	  throw new Error(`Invalid compiler build: ${JSON.stringify(value)}, expected one of: "fast", "small"`)
103	}

4	export class GeneratorRegistry {

10

18	      [...this.#generators.entries()].map(([name, generator]) => [
19	        name,
20	        {
21	          type: 'in-process',
22	          generator,
23	        } satisfies GeneratorRegistryEntry,
24	      ]),
25	    )

549	  client-packages:
550	    name: Client internals and generators [v${{ matrix.node }}]
551	    timeout-minutes: 30
552	    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
553	    if: |
554	      contains(inputs.jobsToRun, '-all-') ||
555	      contains(inputs.jobsToRun, '-client-')
556	    strategy:
557	      fail-fast: false
558	      matrix:
559	        node: ['20.19.0', '22.12.0', '24']
560	    steps:
561	      - uses: actions/checkout@v6
562	
563	      - name: Install & build
564	        uses: ./.github/actions/setup
565	        with:
566	          node-version: ${{ matrix.node }}
567	          engine-hash: ${{ inputs.engineHash }}
568	          skip-tsc: false
569	
570	      - run: pnpm run test
571	        name: 'client-common'
572	        working-directory: packages/client-common
573	
574	      - run: pnpm run test
575	        name: 'client-engine-runtime'
576	        working-directory: packages/client-engine-runtime
577	
578	      - run: pnpm run test
579	        name: 'ts-builders'
580	        working-directory: packages/ts-builders
581	
582	      - run: pnpm run test
583	        name: 'client-generator-js'
584	        working-directory: packages/client-generator-js
585	
586	      - run: pnpm run test
587	        name: 'client-generator-ts'
588	        working-directory: packages/client-generator-ts
589	
590	      - run: pnpm run test
591	        name: 'client-generator-registry'
592	        working-directory: packages/client-generator-registry
593	
594	  #
595	  # WORKSPACE-TYPES
596	  #
597	  workspace-types:
598	    name: Workspace types
599	    timeout-minutes: 15
600	    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
601	    strategy:
602	      fail-fast: false
603	      matrix:
604	        node: ['20.19.0']
605	    steps:
606	      - uses: actions/checkout@v6
607	
608	      - name: Install & build
609	        uses: ./.github/actions/setup
610	        with:
611	          node-version: ${{ matrix.node }}
612	          skip-tsc: true
613	          engine-hash: ${{ inputs.engineHash }}
614	
615	      - run: pnpm tsc -p tsconfig.utils.typecheck.json
616	
617	  #
618	  # INTEGRATION-TESTS
619	  #
620	  integration-tests:
621	    name: Integration tests
622	    timeout-minutes: 20
623	    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
624	    if: |
625	      contains(inputs.jobsToRun, '-all-') ||
626	      contains(inputs.jobsToRun, '-integration-tests-')
627	    strategy:
628	      fail-fast: false
629	      matrix:
630	        node: ['20.19.0']
631	    steps:
632	      - uses: actions/checkout@v6
633	
634	      - name: Start Docker Compose Services
635	        uses: nick-fields/retry@v3
636	        with:
637	          timeout_minutes: 10
638	          max_attempts: 5
639	          retry_wait_seconds: 120
640	          command: docker compose -f docker/docker-compose.yml up --wait --detach postgres mysql mariadb mssql
641	
642	      - name: Install & build
643	        uses: ./.github/actions/setup
644	        with:
645	          node-version: ${{ matrix.node }}
646	          engine-hash: ${{ inputs.engineHash }}
647	          skip-tsc: true

2	 * A JSON-RPC adapter for the `prisma-client-js` generator. It is only used by Studio.
3	 *
4	 * It will be removed in the future.

6	
7	import path from 'node:path'
8	
9	import { dmmfToTypes, externalToInternalDmmf, PrismaClientJsGenerator } from '@prisma/client-generator-js'
10	import { generatorHandler } from '@prisma/generator-helper'
11	
12	export { dmmfToTypes, externalToInternalDmmf }
13	
14	if (process.argv[1] === __filename) {
15	  const generator = new PrismaClientJsGenerator({
16	    shouldResolvePrismaClient: false,
17	    runtimePath: path.join(__dirname, '..', 'runtime'),
18	  })
19	
20	  generatorHandler({
21	    onManifest(config) {
22	      return generator.getManifest(config)
23	    },
24	
25	    onGenerate(options) {
26	      return generator.generate(options)
27	    },
28	  })
29	}

## Comprehensive Prisma Generator Packages Structure Report

Based on my exploration of the monorepo, here's the complete structure for creating new Prisma generator packages:

2. **@prisma/client-generator-js** - JavaScript client generator
3. **@prisma/client-generator-ts** - TypeScript client generator

```typescript
export type Generator = {
```

## Prisma Monorepo Package Setup Patterns - Complete Analysis

I've thoroughly explored the Prisma monorepo and documented all patterns for creating new packages. Here's the complete breakdown:

### 1. BUILD SYSTEM - `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775470169838-18irwj/helpers/compile/`

**Key Files:**
- `helpers/compile/build.ts` - Main build orchestrator
- `helpers/compile/configs.ts` - Config pattern templates

**Build Configuration Patterns:**

Three main config patterns are available in `configs.ts`:

1. **`adapterConfig`** - For bundled single-entry packages
   - Bundles `src/index.ts` to `dist/index`
   - Creates both `.js` (CJS) and `.mjs` (ESM) outputs
   - Emits `.d.ts` and `.d.mts` type definitions
   - Used by: `@prisma/debug`, `@prisma/client-generator-js`, `@prisma/client-generator-ts`

2. **`unbundledConfig`** - For non-bundled multi-file packages
   - Processes all `src/**/*.ts` files

249	
250	    // 3. Download all binaries and binary targets needed
251	    const neededVersions = Object.create(null)
252	    for (const g of generators) {
253	      if (
254	        g.manifest &&
255	        g.manifest.requiresEngines &&
256	        Array.isArray(g.manifest.requiresEngines) &&
257	        g.manifest.requiresEngines.length > 0
258	      ) {
259	        const neededVersion = getEngineVersionForGenerator(g.manifest, version)
260	        if (!neededVersions[neededVersion]) {
261	          neededVersions[neededVersion] = { engines: [], binaryTargets: [] }
262	        }
263	
264	        for (const engine of g.manifest.requiresEngines) {
265	          if (!neededVersions[neededVersion].engines.includes(engine)) {
266	            neededVersions[neededVersion].engines.push(engine)
267	          }
268	        }
269	
270	        const generatorBinaryTargets = g.options?.generator?.binaryTargets
271	
272	        if (generatorBinaryTargets && generatorBinaryTargets.length > 0) {
273	          for (const binaryTarget of generatorBinaryTargets) {
274	            if (!neededVersions[neededVersion].binaryTargets.find((object) => object.value === binaryTarget.value)) {
275	              neededVersions[neededVersion].binaryTargets.push(binaryTarget)
276	            }
277	          }
278	        }
279	      }
280	    }
281	
282	    debug('neededVersions', JSON.stringify(neededVersions, null, 2))
283	    const { binaryPathsByVersion } = await getBinaryPathsByVersion({
284	      neededVersions,
285	      // We're lazily computing the binary target here, to avoid printing the
286	      // `Prisma failed to detect the libssl/openssl version to use` warning
287	      // on StackBlitz, where the binary target is not detected.
288	      //
289	      // On other platforms, it's safe and fast to call this function again,
290	      // as its result is memoized anyway.
291	      detectBinaryTarget: getBinaryTargetForCurrentPlatform,
292	      version,
293	      printDownloadProgress,
294	      skipDownload,
295	      binaryPathsOverride,
296	    })
297	    for (const generator of generators) {
298	      if (generator.manifest && generator.manifest.requiresEngines) {
299	        const engineVersion = getEngineVersionForGenerator(generator.manifest, version)
300	        const binaryPaths = binaryPathsByVersion[engineVersion]
301	        // pick only the engines that we need for this generator
302	        const generatorBinaryPaths = pick(binaryPaths ?? {}, generator.manifest.requiresEngines)
303	        debug({ generatorBinaryPaths })
304	        generator.setBinaryPaths(generatorBinaryPaths)
305	      }
306	    }
307	
308	    return generators
309	  } catch (e) {
310	    // make sure all generators that are already running are being stopped
311	    runningGenerators.forEach((g) => g.stop())
312	    throw e
313	  }
314	}
315	
316	type NeededVersions = {
317	  [key: string]: {
318	    engines: EngineType[]
319	    binaryTargets: BinaryTargetsEnvValue[]
320	  }
321	}
322	
323	export type GetBinaryPathsByVersionInput = {
324	  neededVersions: NeededVersions
325	  detectBinaryTarget: () => Promise<BinaryTarget>
326	  version?: string
327	  printDownloadProgress?: boolean
328	  skipDownload?: boolean
329	  binaryPathsOverride?: BinaryPathsOverride
330	}
331	
332	/**
333	 * Shortcut for getGenerators, if there is only one generator defined. Useful for testing.
334	 * @param schemaPath path to schema.prisma
335	 * @param aliases Aliases like `photonjs` -> `node_modules/photonjs/gen.js`
336	 * @param version Version of the binary, commit hash of https://github.com/prisma/prisma-engine/commits/master
337	 * @param printDownloadProgress `boolean` to print download progress or not
338	 */
339	export async function getGenerator(options: GetGeneratorOptions): Promise<Generator> {
340	  const generators = await getGenerators(options)
341	  return generators[0]
342	}
343	
344	export function skipIndex<T = any>(arr: T[], index: number): T[] {
345	  return [...arr.slice(0, index), ...arr.slice(index + 1)]
346	}
347	
348	export const knownBinaryTargets: BinaryTarget[] = [...binaryTargets, 'native']
349	
350	const oldToNewBinaryTargetsMapping = {
351	  'linux-glibc-libssl1.0.1': 'debian-openssl-1.0.x',
352	  'linux-glibc-libssl1.0.2': 'debian-openssl-1.0.x',
353	  'linux-glibc-libssl1.1.0': 'debian-openssl1.1.x',
354	}
355	
356	async function validateGenerators(generators: GeneratorConfig[]): Promise<void> {
357	  const binaryTarget = await getBinaryTargetForCurrentPlatform()
358	
359	  for (const generator of generators) {
360	    if (generator.binaryTargets) {
361	      const binaryTargets =
362	        generator.binaryTargets && generator.binaryTargets.length > 0
363	          ? generator.binaryTargets
364	          : [{ fromEnvVar: null, value: 'native' }]
365	
366	      const resolvedBinaryTargets: string[] = binaryTargets
367	        .flatMap((object) => parseBinaryTargetsEnvValue(object))
368	        .map((p) => (p === 'native' ? binaryTarget : p))
369	
370	      for (const resolvedBinaryTarget of resolvedBinaryTargets) {
371	        if (oldToNewBinaryTargetsMapping[resolvedBinaryTarget]) {
372	          throw new Error(
373	            `Binary target ${red(bold(resolvedBinaryTarget))} is deprecated. Please use ${green(
374	              bold(oldToNewBinaryTargetsMapping[resolvedBinaryTarget]),
375	            )} instead.`,
376	          )
377	        }
378	        if (!knownBinaryTargets.includes(resolvedBinaryTarget as BinaryTarget)) {
379	          throw new Error(
380	            `Unknown binary target ${red(resolvedBinaryTarget)} in generator ${bold(generator.name)}.
381	Possible binaryTargets: ${green(knownBinaryTargets.join(', '))}`,
382	          )
383	        }
384	      }
385	
386	      // Only show warning if resolvedBinaryTargets
387	      // is missing current platform
388	      if (!resolvedBinaryTargets.includes(binaryTarget)) {
389	        const originalBinaryTargetsConfig = getOriginalBinaryTargetsValue(generator.binaryTargets)
390	
391	        console.log(`${yellow('Warning:')} Your current platform \`${bold(
392	          binaryTarget,
393	        )}\` is not included in your generator's \`binaryTargets\` configuration ${JSON.stringify(
394	          originalBinaryTargetsConfig,
395	        )}.
396	To fix it, use this generator config in your ${bold('schema.prisma')}:
397	${green(
398	  printGeneratorConfig({
399	    ...generator,
400	    binaryTargets: fixBinaryTargets(generator.binaryTargets, binaryTarget),
401	  }),
402	)}
403	${gray(
404	  `Note, that by providing \`native\`, Prisma Client automatically resolves \`${binaryTarget}\`.
405	Read more about deploying Prisma Client: ${underline(
406	    'https://www.prisma.io/docs/reference/tools-and-interfaces/prisma-schema/generators',
407	  )}`,
408	)}\n`)
409	      }
410	    }
411	  }
412	}
413	
414	function filterGenerators(generators: GeneratorConfig[], generatorNames: string[]) {
415	  if (generatorNames.length < 1) {
416	    return generators
417	  }
418	
419	  const filtered = generators.filter((generator) => generatorNames.includes(generator.name))
420	
421	  if (filtered.length !== generatorNames.length) {
422	    const missings = generatorNames.filter((name) => filtered.find((generator) => generator.name === name) == null)
423	    const isSingular = missings.length <= 1
424	    throw new Error(
425	      `The ${isSingular ? 'generator' : 'generators'} ${bold(missings.join(', '))} specified via ${bold(
426	        '--generator',
427	      )} ${isSingular ? 'does' : 'do'} not exist in your Prisma schema`,
428	    )
429	  }
430	
431	  return filtered
432	}
433

Perfect! Now I have a comprehensive understanding. Let me create a final summary document.

## Prisma Generator Architecture - Complete Exploration Report

I've thoroughly explored the Prisma monorepo's generator system. Here's a comprehensive breakdown:

### 1. **Core Generator Packages**

#### `/packages/generator/` - Generator Configuration Types
- **Package.json**: `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775470169838-18irwj/packages/generator/package.json`
- **Key Files**:
  - `src/configuration.ts` - Defines `GeneratorConfig`, `GeneratorOptions`, `GeneratorManifest`, `EnvValue`, `BinaryTargetsEnvValue`, `ConnectorType`, `DataSource`
  - `src/generator.ts` - Defines `Generator` interface with `getManifest()` and `generate()` methods
  - `src/typedSql.ts` - Defines `SqlQueryOutput`, `SqlQueryParameterOutput`, `SqlQueryColumnOutput`, `QueryIntrospectionType`

#### `/packages/dmmf/` - Data Model Meta Format Types
- **Package.json**: `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775470169838-18irwj/packages/dmmf/package.json`
- **Key Files**:
  - `src/dmmf.ts` - Complete DMMF schema definitions including:
    - `Document` - Root type containing `Datamodel`, `Schema`, `Mappings`
    - `Model` - Fields, uniqueFields, uniqueIndexes, primaryKey, documentation, isGenerated
    - `Field` - kind (scalar/object/enum/unsupported), type, isRequired, isList, isUnique, isId, nativeType, relationFromFields, etc.
    - `DatamodelEnum` - enum definitions with values
    - `Datamodel` - models, enums, types, indexes
    - `Schema` - GraphQL schema with rootQueryType, rootMutationType, inputObjectTypes, outputObjectTypes, enumTypes, fieldRefTypes
    - `ModelMapping` - maps models to GraphQL operations
  - `src/convert.ts` - `datamodelEnumToSchemaEnum()` utility
  - `src/util.ts` - `ReadonlyDeep<T>` utility type

#### `/packages/generator-helper/` - Generator Runtime/IPC Protocol
- **Package.json**: `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775470169838-18irwj/packages/generator-helper/package.json`
- **Key Files**:
  - `src/generatorHandler.ts` - **CORE**: Implements JSON-RPC IPC protocol
    - `Handler` interface with `onGenerate(options)` and optional `onManifest(config)`
    - `generatorHandler(handler)` function - stdin/stdout readline-based JSON-RPC server
    - Listens for `generate` and `getManifest` JSON-RPC method calls
    - Sends responses via stderr
  - `src/GeneratorProcess.ts` - Client-side process manager
    - `GeneratorProcess` class manages generator subprocess (fork for Node, spawn otherwise)
    - `GeneratorError` class for error handling
    - `getManifest()` and `generate()` RPC methods
    - Handles SIGTERM/SIGKILL lifecycle
  - `src/json-rpc.ts` - JSON-RPC 2.0 protocol types: `Request`, `SuccessResponse`, `ErrorResponse`
  - `src/index.ts` - Re-exports generatorHandler, GeneratorError, GeneratorProcess, and types from @prisma/generator and @prisma/dmmf for backwards compatibility

### 2. **Key Type Definitions**

**GeneratorConfig** (from `/packages/generator/src/configuration.ts`):
```typescript
{
  name: string
  output: EnvValue | null
  isCustomOutput?: boolean
  provider: EnvValue
  config: Record<string, string | string[] | undefined>  // custom config
  binaryTargets: BinaryTargetsEnvValue[]
  previewFeatures: string[]
  sourceFilePath: string
}
```

**GeneratorOptions** (passed to `onGenerate`):
```typescript
{
  generator: GeneratorConfig
  otherGenerators: GeneratorConfig[]
  schemaPath: string
  dmmf: DMMF.Document
  datasources: DataSource[]
  datamodel: string
  version: string
  binaryPaths?: BinaryPaths
  noHints?: boolean
  allowNoModels?: boolean
  typedSql?: SqlQueryOutput[]
}
```

**GeneratorManifest** (returned by `onManifest`):
```typescript
{
  prettyName?: string
  defaultOutput?: string
  denylists?: {
    models?: string[]
    fields?: string[]
  }
  requiresGenerators?: string[]  // e.g., ['prisma-client-js']
  requiresEngines?: EngineType[]  // e.g., ['schemaEngine']
  version?: string
  requiresEngineVersion?: string
}
```

**DMMF.Field** (core data model):
```typescript
{
  kind: 'scalar' | 'object' | 'enum' | 'unsupported'
  name: string
  type: string  // e.g., 'String', 'Int', '$User'
  isRequired: boolean
  isList: boolean
  isUnique: boolean
  isId: boolean
  isReadOnly: boolean
  isGenerated?: boolean
  isUpdatedAt?: boolean
  nativeType?: [string, string[]]  // e.g., ['VarChar', ['191']]
  dbName?: string | null
  hasDefaultValue: boolean
  default?: FieldDefault | FieldDefaultScalar | FieldDefaultScalar[]
  relationFromFields?: string[]
  relationToFields?: string[]
  relationOnDelete?: string
  relationOnUpdate?: string
  relationName?: string
  documentation?: string
}
```

### 3. **Generator Implementation Patterns**

**Pattern 1: In-Process Generator** (TypeScript/JavaScript class):
```typescript
// Location: /packages/client-generator-js/src/generator.ts
export class PrismaClientJsGenerator implements Generator {
  readonly name = 'prisma-client-js'
  
  async getManifest(config: GeneratorConfig): Promise<GeneratorManifest> {
    return {
      defaultOutput: '.prisma/client',
      prettyName: 'Prisma Client',
      version: clientVersion,
      requiresEngines: [],
      requiresEngineVersion: enginesVersion,
    }
  }
  
  async generate(options: GeneratorOptions): Promise<void> {
    // Implementation
  }
}
```

**Pattern 2: JSON-RPC Generator** (Standalone executable):
```javascript
// Location: /packages/cli/src/__tests__/fixtures/dependent-generator/generator.js
const { generatorHandler } = require('@prisma/generator-helper')

generatorHandler({
  onManifest() {
    return {
      defaultOutput: 'my-generator-output',
      prettyName: 'My Generator',
      requiresEngines: [],
      requiresGenerators: ['prisma-client-js'],
    }
  },
  async onGenerate(options) {
    // Implementation
  },
})
```

### 4. **Generator Lifecycle & Invocation**

**File**: `/packages/internals/src/get-generators/getGenerators.ts` (lines 150-225)

1. **Registry Lookup**: Generator provider name matched against `GeneratorRegistry`
2. **Instantiation**: Creates either `JsonRpcGenerator` (RPC-based) or `InProcessGenerator` (class-based)
3. **Init**: Calls `generator.init()` which fetches manifest
4. **Output Resolution**: Resolves output directory using manifest's `defaultOutput`
5. **Options Creation**: Prepares `GeneratorOptions` with DMMF, datasources, etc.
6. **Dependency Validation**: Checks `requiresGenerators` manifest property
7. **Binary Download**: Downloads engines if `requiresEngines` specified
8. **Generation**: Calls `generator.generate(options)`

### 5. **Generator Management Classes**

**Abstract Generator** (at `/packages/internals/src/Generator.ts`):
- Base class with `manifest`, `config`, `options` properties
- Methods: `init()`, `setOptions()`, `setBinaryPaths()`, `generate()`, `stop()`

**JsonRpcGenerator** (child class):
- Uses `GeneratorProcess` to manage subprocess
- Communicates via JSON-RPC 2.0 on stdin/stderr

**InProcessGenerator** (child class):
- Directly calls `Generator` interface implementation
- No subprocess overhead

### 6. **JSON-RPC Communication Protocol**

**stdin**: Receives JSON-RPC requests
```json
{"jsonrpc":"2.0","method":"generate","params":{...GeneratorOptions...},"id":1}
{"jsonrpc":"2.0","method":"getManifest","params":{...GeneratorConfig...},"id":2}
```

**stderr**: Sends JSON-RPC responses
```json
{"jsonrpc":"2.0","result":null,"id":1}
{"jsonrpc":"2.0","result":{"manifest":{...}},"id":2}
{"jsonrpc":"2.0","error":{"code":-32000,"message":"Error message","data":{"stack":"..."}},"id":3}
```

### 7. **File Paths Summary**

**Core Type Definitions**:
- `/packages/generator/src/configuration.ts` - GeneratorConfig, GeneratorOptions, GeneratorManifest
- `/packages/dmmf/src/dmmf.ts` - DMMF.Document, Model, Field, etc.
- `/packages/generator/src/typedSql.ts` - SqlQueryOutput types

**Generator Protocol**:
- `/packages/generator-helper/src/generatorHandler.ts` - generatorHandler function
- `/packages/generator-helper/src/GeneratorProcess.ts` - GeneratorProcess client
- `/packages/generator-helper/src/json-rpc.ts` - JSON-RPC 2.0 types

**Generator Management**:
- `/packages/internals/src/Generator.ts` - Generator base classes
- `/packages/internals/src/get-generators/getGenerators.ts` - Generator factory & lifecycle

**Built-in Generator Example**:
- `/packages/client-generator-js/src/generator.ts` - PrismaClientJsGenerator implementation

**Tests/Examples**:
- `/packages/generator-helper/src/__tests__/minimal-executable` - Minimal generator
- `/packages/cli/src/__tests__/fixtures/dependent-generator/generator.js` - Generator with dependencies

### 8. **Key Utilities & Helpers**

- `/packages/internals/src/get-generators/utils/getBinaryPathsByVersion.ts` - Engine binary resolution
- `/packages/internals/src/get-generators/utils/printGeneratorConfig.ts` - Config formatting
- `/packages/generator-helper/src/__tests__/` - Test executables showing generator patterns

This architecture allows generators to be either in-process (fast, for built-in generators) or external processes (flexible, for community generators), with a clean JSON-RPC protocol for inter-process communication.

```json
{"jsonrpc":"2.0","error":{"code":-32000,"message":"Error message","data":{"stack":"..."}},"id":3}
```

### 7. **File Paths Summary**

**Core Type Definitions**:
- `/packages/generator/src/configuration.ts` - GeneratorConfig, GeneratorOptions, GeneratorManifest
- `/packages/dmmf/src/dmmf.ts` - DMMF.Document, Model, Field, etc.
- `/packages/generator/src/typedSql.ts` - SqlQueryOutput types

**Generator Protocol**:
- `/packages/generator-helper/src/generatorHandler.ts` - generatorHandler function
- `/packages/generator-helper/src/GeneratorProcess.ts` - GeneratorProcess client
- `/packages/generator-helper/src/json-rpc.ts` - JSON-RPC 2.0 types

**Generator Management**:
- `/packages/internals/src/Generator.ts` - Generator base classes
- `/packages/internals/src/get-generators/getGenerators.ts` - Generator factory & lifecycle

**Built-in Generator Example**:
- `/packages/client-generator-js/src/generator.ts` - PrismaClientJsGenerator implementation

**Tests/Examples**:
- `/packages/generator-helper/src/__tests__/minimal-executable` - Minimal generator
- `/packages/cli/src/__tests__/fixtures/dependent-generator/generator.js` - Generator with dependencies

### 8. **Key Utilities & Helpers**

- `/packages/internals/src/get-generators/utils/getBinaryPathsByVersion.ts` - Engine binary resolution
- `/packages/internals/src/get-generators/utils/printGeneratorConfig.ts` - Config formatting
- `/packages/generator-helper/src/__tests__/` - Test executables showing generator patterns

This architecture allows generators to be either in-process (fast, for built-in generators) or external processes (flexible, for community generators), with a clean JSON-RPC protocol for inter-process communication.

import { ReadonlyDeep } from './util'

export type Document = ReadonlyDeep<{
  datamodel: Datamodel
  schema: Schema
  mappings: Mappings
}>

export type Mappings = ReadonlyDeep<{
  modelOperations: ModelMapping[]
  otherOperations: {
    read: string[]
    write: string[]
  }
}>

export type OtherOperationMappings = ReadonlyDeep<{
  read: string[]
  write: string[]
}>

export type DatamodelEnum = ReadonlyDeep<{
  name: string
  values: EnumValue[]
  dbName?: string | null
  documentation?: string
}>

export type SchemaEnum = ReadonlyDeep<{
  name: string
  values: string[]
}>

export type EnumValue = ReadonlyDeep<{
  name: string
  dbName: string | null
}>

export type Datamodel = ReadonlyDeep<{
  models: Model[]
  enums: DatamodelEnum[]
  types: Model[]
  indexes: Index[]
}>

export type uniqueIndex = ReadonlyDeep<{
  name: string
  fields: string[]
}>
export type PrimaryKey = ReadonlyDeep<{
  name: string | null
  fields: string[]
}>
export type Model = ReadonlyDeep<{
  name: string
  dbName: string | null
  schema: string | null
  fields: Field[]
  uniqueFields: string[][]
  uniqueIndexes: uniqueIndex[]
  documentation?: string
  primaryKey: PrimaryKey | null
  isGenerated?: boolean
}>

export type FieldKind = 'scalar' | 'object' | 'enum' | 'unsupported'

export type FieldNamespace = 'model' | 'prisma'
export type FieldLocation = 'scalar' | 'inputObjectTypes' | 'outputObjectTypes' | 'enumTypes' | 'fieldRefTypes'

export type Field = ReadonlyDeep<{
  kind: FieldKind
  name: string
  isRequired: boolean
  isList: boolean
  isUnique: boolean
  isId: boolean
  isReadOnly: boolean
  isGenerated?: boolean // does not exist on 'type' but does on 'model'
  isUpdatedAt?: boolean // does not exist on 'type' but does on 'model'
  /**
   * Describes the data type in the same the way it is defined in the Prisma schema:
   * BigInt, Boolean, Bytes, DateTime, Decimal, Float, Int, JSON, String, $ModelName
   */
  type: string
  /**
   * Native database type, if specified.
   * For example, `@db.VarChar(191)` is encoded as `['VarChar', ['191']]`,
   * `@db.Text` is encoded as `['Text', []]`.
   */
  nativeType?: [string, string[]] | null
  dbName?: string | null
  hasDefaultValue: boolean
  default?: FieldDefault | FieldDefaultScalar | FieldDefaultScalar[]
  relationFromFields?: string[]
  relationToFields?: string[]
  relationOnDelete?: string
  relationOnUpdate?: string
  relationName?: string
  documentation?: string
}>

export type FieldDefault = ReadonlyDeep<{
  name: string
  args: Array<string | number>
}>

export type FieldDefaultScalar = string | boolean | number

export type Index = ReadonlyDeep<{
  model: string
  type: IndexType
  isDefinedOnField: boolean
  name?: string
  dbName?: string
  algorithm?: string
  clustered?: boolean
  fields: IndexField[]
}>

export type IndexType = 'id' | 'normal' | 'unique' | 'fulltext'

export type IndexField = ReadonlyDeep<{
  name: string
  sortOrder?: SortOrder
  length?: number
  operatorClass?: string
}>

export type SortOrder = 'asc' | 'desc'

export type Schema = ReadonlyDeep<{
  rootQueryType?: string
  rootMutationType?: string
  inputObjectTypes: {
    // For now there are no `model` InputTypes
    model?: InputType[]
    prisma?: InputType[]
  }
  outputObjectTypes: {
    model: OutputType[]
    prisma: OutputType[]
  }
  enumTypes: {
    model?: SchemaEnum[]
    prisma: SchemaEnum[]
  }
  fieldRefTypes: {
    prisma?: FieldRefType[]
  }
}>

export type Query = ReadonlyDeep<{
  name: string
  args: SchemaArg[]
  output: QueryOutput
}>

export type QueryOutput = ReadonlyDeep<{
  name: string
  isRequired: boolean
  isList: boolean
}>

export type TypeRef<AllowedLocations extends FieldLocation> = {
  isList: boolean
  type: string
  location: AllowedLocations
  namespace?: FieldNamespace
}

export type InputTypeRef = TypeRef<'scalar' | 'inputObjectTypes' | 'enumTypes' | 'fieldRefTypes'>

export type SchemaArg = ReadonlyDeep<{
  name: string
  comment?: string
  isNullable: boolean
  isRequired: boolean
  inputTypes: InputTypeRef[]
  isParameterizable: boolean
  requiresOtherFields?: string[]
  deprecation?: Deprecation
}>

export type OutputType = ReadonlyDeep<{
  name: string
  fields: SchemaField[]
}>

export type SchemaField = ReadonlyDeep<{
  name: string
  isNullable?: boolean
  outputType: OutputTypeRef
  args: SchemaArg[]
  deprecation?: Deprecation
  documentation?: string
}>

export type OutputTypeRef = TypeRef<'scalar' | 'outputObjectTypes' | 'enumTypes'>

export type Deprecation = ReadonlyDeep<{
  sinceVersion: string
  reason: string
  plannedRemovalVersion?: string
}>

export type InputType = ReadonlyDeep<{
  name: string
  constraints: {
    maxNumFields: number | null
    minNumFields: number | null
    fields?: string[]
  }
  meta?: {
    source?: string
    grouping?: string
  }
  fields: SchemaArg[]
}>

export type FieldRefType = ReadonlyDeep<{
  name: string
  allowTypes: FieldRefAllowType[]
  fields: SchemaArg[]
}>

export type FieldRefAllowType = TypeRef<'scalar' | 'enumTypes'>

export type ModelMapping = ReadonlyDeep<{
  model: string
  plural: string
  findUnique?: string | null
  findUniqueOrThrow?: string | null
  findFirst?: string | null
  findFirstOrThrow?: string | null
  findMany?: string | null
  create?: string | null
  createMany?: string | null
  createManyAndReturn?: string | null
  update?: string | null
  updateMany?: string | null
  updateManyAndReturn?: string | null
  upsert?: string | null
  delete?: string | null
  deleteMany?: string | null
  aggregate?: string | null
  groupBy?: string | null
  count?: string | null
  findRaw?: string | null
  aggregateRaw?: string | null
}>

export enum ModelAction {
  findUnique = 'findUnique',
  findUniqueOrThrow = 'findUniqueOrThrow',
  findFirst = 'findFirst',
  findFirstOrThrow = 'findFirstOrThrow',
  findMany = 'findMany',
  create = 'create',
  createMany = 'createMany',
  createManyAndReturn = 'createManyAndReturn',
  update = 'update',
  updateMany = 'updateMany',
  updateManyAndReturn = 'updateManyAndReturn',
  upsert = 'upsert',
  delete = 'delete',
  deleteMany = 'deleteMany',
  groupBy = 'groupBy',
  count = 'count', // TODO: count does not actually exist in DMMF
  aggregate = 'aggregate',
  findRaw = 'findRaw',
  aggregateRaw = 'aggregateRaw',
}

1	import path from 'node:path'
2	
3	import { enginesVersion } from '@prisma/engines-version'
4	import { Generator, GeneratorConfig, GeneratorManifest, GeneratorOptions } from '@prisma/generator'
5	import { BuiltInProvider, parseEnvValue } from '@prisma/internals'
6	
7	import { version as clientVersion } from '../package.json'
8	import { generateClient } from './generateClient'
9	import { resolvePrismaClient } from './resolvePrismaClient'
10	
11	type PrismaClientJsGeneratorOptions = {
12	  shouldResolvePrismaClient?: boolean
13	  shouldInstallMissingPackages?: boolean
14	  runtimePath?: string
15	}
16	
17	// const MISSING_CUSTOM_OUTPUT_PATH_WARNING = `\
18	// ${yellow('Warning:')} You did not specify an output path for your \`generator\` in schema.prisma. \
19	// This behavior is deprecated and will no longer be supported in Prisma 7.0.0. To learn more \
20	// visit https://pris.ly/cli/output-path`
21	
22	export class PrismaClientJsGenerator implements Generator {
23	  readonly name = BuiltInProvider.PrismaClientJs
24	
25	  #shouldResolvePrismaClient: boolean
26	  #runtimePath?: string
27	  #cachedPrismaClientPath: string | undefined
28	
29	  constructor({ shouldResolvePrismaClient = true, runtimePath }: PrismaClientJsGeneratorOptions = {}) {
30	    this.#shouldResolvePrismaClient = shouldResolvePrismaClient
31	    this.#runtimePath = runtimePath
32	  }
33	
34	  async getManifest(config: GeneratorConfig): Promise<GeneratorManifest> {
35	    // TODO: warning disabled for now until we fixed issues around custom output paths - see ORM-976
36	    // if (!config.output) {
37	    //   console.warn(MISSING_CUSTOM_OUTPUT_PATH_WARNING)
38	    // }
39	
40	    // If `this.#shouldResolvePrismaClient` is true, which is normally the case,
41	    // we find the default output path by resolving the path to the
42	    // `@prisma/client` package. While we resolve the absolute path to the
43	    // package itself, the generator will rewrite it to replace
44	    // `.../@prisma/client` with `.../.prisma/client`, as long as
45	    // `config.isCustomOutput` is false.
46	    //
47	    // If a custom output path is provided, then the default output path doesn't
48	    // matter and we could use a static value here, but that unfortunately
49	    // wouldn't allow us to avoid resolving the client directory and would only
50	    // delay that until the `generate` method call because we copy the runtime
51	    // files if a custom output path is used, and we also copy the WASM modules
52	    // regardles of the output path. Not copying the runtime files is a breaking
53	    // change, so we can only completely get rid of this behaviour in the new
54	    // generator.
55	    //
56	    // If `this.#shouldResolvePrismaClient` is false, which is the case when
57	    // using the JSON-RPC compatibility adapter for Prisma Studio, we should use
58	    // a static value here.
59	    const defaultOutput = this.#shouldResolvePrismaClient ? await this.#getPrismaClientPath(config) : '.prisma/client'
60	
61	    return {
62	      defaultOutput,
63	      prettyName: 'Prisma Client',
64	      version: clientVersion,
65	      requiresEngines: [],
66	      requiresEngineVersion: enginesVersion,
67	    }
68	  }
69	
70	  async generate(options: GeneratorOptions): Promise<void> {
71	    const outputDir = parseEnvValue(options.generator.output!)
72	
73	    await generateClient({
74	      datamodel: options.datamodel,
75	      schemaPath: options.schemaPath,
76	      binaryPaths: options.binaryPaths!,
77	      datasources: options.datasources,
78	      outputDir,
79	      copyRuntime: Boolean(options.generator.config.copyRuntime),
80	      copyRuntimeSourceMaps: Boolean(process.env.PRISMA_COPY_RUNTIME_SOURCEMAPS),
81	      runtimeSourcePath: await this.#getRuntimePath(options.generator),
82	      dmmf: options.dmmf,
83	      generator: options.generator,
84	      engineVersion: options.version,
85	      clientVersion,
86	      activeProvider: options.datasources[0]?.activeProvider,
87	      typedSql: options.typedSql,
88	      compilerBuild: parseCompilerBuildFromUnknown(options.generator.config.compilerBuild),
89	    })
90	  }
91	
92	  async #getPrismaClientPath(config: GeneratorConfig): Promise<string> {
93	    if (this.#cachedPrismaClientPath) {
94	      return this.#cachedPrismaClientPath
95	    }
96	
97	    this.#cachedPrismaClientPath = await resolvePrismaClient(path.dirname(config.sourceFilePath))
98	    return this.#cachedPrismaClientPath
99	  }
100	
101	  async #getRuntimePath(config: GeneratorConfig): Promise<string> {
102	    if (this.#runtimePath) {
103	      return this.#runtimePath
104	    }
105	
106	    this.#runtimePath = path.join(await this.#getPrismaClientPath(config), 'runtime')
107	    return this.#runtimePath
108	  }
109	}
110	
111	function parseCompilerBuildFromUnknown(value: unknown): 'fast' | 'small' {
112	  if (value === undefined) {
113	    return 'fast'
114	  }
115	  if (value === 'small' || value === 'fast') {
116	    return value
117	  }
118	  throw new Error(`Invalid compiler build: ${JSON.stringify(value)}, expected one of: "fast", "small"`)
119	}
120

defaultRegistry.add(new PrismaClientJsGenerator())

const tsGenerator = new PrismaClientTsGenerator()
defaultRegistry.add(tsGenerator)
defaultRegistry.addAliased('prisma-client', tsGenerator)

content.thinking:
Now I have all the information I need to create a detailed implementation plan. Let me now design the plan and write it to the plan file.

Here's what the new package needs:

**Package name**: `@prisma/rest-client-generator` (or `@prisma/client-generator-rest`)
Let me think about the naming convention. The existing generators are:
- `@prisma/client-generator-js`
- `@prisma/client-generator-ts`
- `@prisma/client-generator-registry`

So the naming pattern is `@prisma/client-generator-*`. 

I'll stick with `@prisma/client-generator-rest` to maintain consistency with the existing naming convention. For each model in the DMMF, I need to generate TypeScript interfaces and CRUD methods that the REST client can use.

Now I'm mapping out the package structure with separate modules for generating interfaces, CRUD methods, and handling type conversions from DMMF scalar types to TypeScript.

The `RestClientGenerator` class will implement the Prisma generator interface with a manifest declaring the plugin name and output directory, then generate TypeScript files containing exported interfaces for each model along with their CRUD operation methods.

Generating the TypeScript interfaces and client methods... Completing the RestClient class with PostClient initialization, then documenting the type mappings for how DMMF scalars translate to TypeScript types and the REST endpoint conventions that map to the generated methods.

Now I'm reconsidering whether to register this generator in the client-generator-registry—the task asks me to follow the monorepo structure, but looking at that registry's purpose, it seems designed specifically for Prisma's built-in generators rather than optional community generators like a REST client.

I think the cleaner approach is to make this a standalone package that users can reference directly in their `prisma.config.ts` without needing registry integration, which aligns better with how the standard Prisma generator protocol works.

Now I'm thinking through the implementation details—I need to handle the `Generator` interface properly, parse the output directory from the generator options, and then work with the DMMF to understand field types. The DMMF gives me different field kinds like scalars, object relations, enums, and unsupported types, so I'll need to handle each appropriately when generating the REST client code.

For the package structure, I'm settling on a simpler layout with core files for the generator itself, orchestration logic, type rendering, client rendering, and a scalar type mapping utility. I'll also include basic tests to verify the generation works.

Now I'm thinking through what the generated code should look like for a concrete example...

Generating TypeScript interfaces... Writing REST client methods... I'm defining the top-level RestClient class that aggregates the UserRestClient and PostRestClient as properties, so they're accessible through a single entry point. Now I'm thinking through the WhereUniqueInput type—it should only include fields marked as id or unique, and for CreateInput I need to exclude generated and read-only fields to avoid passing values that the server handles automatically. For `UpdateInput`, all scalar fields should be optional since updates are partial. The main interface needs all fields (scalar and relation references), while `CreateInput` contains only non-generated scalar fields with proper required/optional distinction, and `UpdateInput` makes those same scalar fields all optional. For the CRUD methods themselves, I need `findMany` to return arrays, `findUnique`/`update`/`delete` to accept unique selectors, and `create` to accept write input—each returning the full model type.

Now I'm thinking through how to structure the input types more carefully: the main interface should include all scalar fields plus optional relation fields, `CreateInput` should require fields without defaults and make others optional while excluding read-only auto-generated fields, and I need to understand what `isReadOnly` means in the DMMF to properly filter those out. For create inputs, fields with defaults become optional even if required in the schema, while update inputs keep all scalars optional. Unique inputs only include ID or unique fields. For the REST endpoints, I'll use a simple pluralization approach—just lowercase the model name and add 's', keeping dependencies minimal rather than pulling in the `pluralize` package.

For the `findUnique` endpoint, the REST pattern extracts the ID from the URL path (like `GET /users/123`), so I need to map that to the `UserWhereUniqueInput` by using the primary key field value.

The REST API routes follow a standard pattern: `GET /users` for listing, `GET /users/:id` for fetching one, `POST /users` for creating, `PUT /users/:id` for updating, and `DELETE /users/:id` for deleting.

Now I'm mapping out the package structure and implementation plan for the REST client generator. Now I'm ready to write out this plan to the file.

content.text: Now I have all the information I need. Let me design the implementation plan with the Plan agent, then write the final plan file.

input.description: Design REST client generator implementation plan
input.prompt:
I need a detailed step-by-step implementation plan for a new Prisma generator package called `@prisma/client-generator-rest` that generates a typed REST API client from a Prisma schema.

## Context

This is a TypeScript monorepo (Prisma ORM) using pnpm workspaces and Turborepo. All packages live under `packages/`. The existing generator packages follow strict patterns.

## Generator Protocol

Generators implement the `Generator` interface from `@prisma/generator`:
```typescript
export type Generator = {
  readonly name: string
  getManifest(config: GeneratorConfig): Promise<GeneratorManifest>
  generate(options: GeneratorOptions): Promise<void>
}
```

`GeneratorOptions` includes `options.dmmf` (a `DMMF.Document`) and `options.generator.output` (output directory). The output directory is resolved with `parseEnvValue(options.generator.output!)` from `@prisma/internals`.

## DMMF Structure (key types from @prisma/dmmf)

```typescript
// DMMF.Document
{ datamodel: Datamodel, schema: Schema, mappings: Mappings }

// DMMF.Datamodel
{ models: Model[], enums: DatamodelEnum[], types: Model[], indexes: Index[] }

// DMMF.Model
{ name: string, fields: Field[], uniqueFields: string[][], uniqueIndexes: uniqueIndex[], primaryKey: PrimaryKey | null, ... }

// DMMF.Field
{ kind: 'scalar'|'object'|'enum'|'unsupported', name: string, type: string, isRequired: boolean, isList: boolean, isUnique: boolean, isId: boolean, isReadOnly: boolean, hasDefaultValue: boolean, ... }

// DMMF.DatamodelEnum
{ name: string, values: EnumValue[] }  // EnumValue: { name: string, dbName: string | null }
```

## Existing Generator Example

`packages/client-generator-js/src/generator.ts`:
```typescript
export class PrismaClientJsGenerator implements Generator {
  readonly name = BuiltInProvider.PrismaClientJs
  async getManifest(config: GeneratorConfig): Promise<GeneratorManifest> {
    return { defaultOutput: '.prisma/client', prettyName: 'Prisma Client', version: clientVersion, requiresEngines: [] }
  }
  async generate(options: GeneratorOptions): Promise<void> {
    const outputDir = parseEnvValue(options.generator.output!)
    // ... writes files to outputDir
  }
}
```

## Monorepo Package Setup

Every package has:
- `package.json` with `main/module/types/exports` pointing to `dist/`
- `tsconfig.json` that extends `../../tsconfig.json` and includes `./**/*`
- `tsconfig.build.json` that extends `../../tsconfig.build.regular.json` with `outDir: "dist"` and `rootDir: "src"`
- `helpers/build.ts` that calls `void build(adapterConfig)` (for bundled packages) 

### Key existing files to be modified:
- `packages/client-generator-registry/src/default.ts` — registers generators in the default registry
- `packages/client-generator-registry/package.json` — lists generator deps
- `tsconfig.build.bundle.json` — path mappings for editor go-to-definition (currently maps 42 packages)
- `.github/workflows/test-template.yml` — CI test steps (lines 570-592 show the `client-packages` job)

## Requirements for the generated REST client

For each Prisma model, generate:
1. **Main TypeScript interface** - all scalar, enum, and optional relation fields
2. **`ModelCreateInput` interface** - scalar/enum fields; required fields that lack `hasDefaultValue`, others optional
3. **`ModelUpdateInput` interface** - all scalar/enum fields optional (partial update)
4. **`ModelWhereUniqueInput` interface** - only `isId || isUnique` scalar fields, all optional
5. **`ModelRestClient` class** - CRUD methods using native `fetch`:
   - `findMany(params?)` → `GET /models`
   - `findUnique(where)` → `GET /models/:id`
   - `create(data)` → `POST /models`
   - `update(where, data)` → `PUT /models/:id`
   - `delete(where)` → `DELETE /models/:id`
6. **TypeScript enums** for each `DatamodelEnum`
7. **Top-level `RestClient` class** with a property per model delegating to `ModelRestClient`

Scalar type mappings:
- `String` → `string`
- `Int`, `Float`, `Decimal`, `BigInt` → `number`
- `Boolean` → `boolean`
- `DateTime` → `string`
- `Json` → `unknown`
- `Bytes` → `string`
- Object (relation) fields → `ModelName | null` (optional reference)
- Enum fields → `EnumName` (the generated TS enum)

## Design constraints
- File names use kebab-case
- Use native JS private fields (`#field`) not `private` keyword
- No barrel files unless already present
- No useless code comments — only "why" comments, no "what" comments
- Use `node:fs/promises` (specifically `mkdir` and `writeFile`) for file output
- Generated output is a single file: `rest-client.ts` in the specified output directory
- All relation fields are optional in the main interface (`field?: RelatedModel`)
- URL path for a model: lowercase model name + 's' (e.g., `User` → `/users`)
- The `id` for URL path in `findUnique`/`update`/`delete` is derived from the first provided key in the `WhereUniqueInput`

I need a detailed step-by-step implementation plan for a new Prisma generator package called `@prisma/client-generator-rest` that generates a typed REST API client from a Prisma schema.

## Context

This is a TypeScript monorepo (Prisma ORM) using pnpm workspaces and Turborepo. All packages live under `packages/`. The existing generator packages follow strict patterns.

## Generator Protocol

Generators implement the `Generator` interface from `@prisma/generator`:
```typescript
export type Generator = {
  readonly name: string
  getManifest(config: GeneratorConfig): Promise<GeneratorManifest>
  generate(options: GeneratorOptions): Promise<void>
}
```

`GeneratorOptions` includes `options.dmmf` (a `DMMF.Document`) and `options.generator.output` (output directory). The output directory is resolved with `parseEnvValue(options.generator.output!)` from `@prisma/internals`.

## DMMF Structure (key types from @prisma/dmmf)

```typescript
// DMMF.Document
{ datamodel: Datamodel, schema: Schema, mappings: Mappings }

// DMMF.Datamodel
{ models: Model[], enums: DatamodelEnum[], types: Model[], indexes: Index[] }

// DMMF.Model
{ name: string, fields: Field[], uniqueFields: string[][], uniqueIndexes: uniqueIndex[], primaryKey: PrimaryKey | null, ... }

// DMMF.Field
{ kind: 'scalar'|'object'|'enum'|'unsupported', name: string, type: string, isRequired: boolean, isList: boolean, isUnique: boolean, isId: boolean, isReadOnly: boolean, hasDefaultValue: boolean, ... }

// DMMF.DatamodelEnum
{ name: string, values: EnumValue[] }  // EnumValue: { name: string, dbName: string | null }
```

## Existing Generator Example

`packages/client-generator-js/src/generator.ts`:
```typescript
export class PrismaClientJsGenerator implements Generator {
  readonly name = BuiltInProvider.PrismaClientJs
  async getManifest(config: GeneratorConfig): Promise<GeneratorManifest> {
    return { defaultOutput: '.prisma/client', prettyName: 'Prisma Client', version: clientVersion, requiresEngines: [] }
  }

    const outputDir = parseEnvValue(options.generator.output!)

  }
}
```

1	import path from 'node:path'
2	
3	import { enginesVersion } from '@prisma/engines-version'
4	import { Generator, GeneratorConfig, GeneratorManifest, GeneratorOptions } from '@prisma/generator'
5	import { BuiltInProvider, parseEnvValue } from '@prisma/internals'
6	
7	import { version as clientVersion } from '../package.json'
8	import { generateClient } from './generateClient'
9	import { resolvePrismaClient } from './resolvePrismaClient'
10	
11	type PrismaClientJsGeneratorOptions = {
12	  shouldResolvePrismaClient?: boolean
13	  shouldInstallMissingPackages?: boolean
14	  runtimePath?: string
15	}
16	
17	// const MISSING_CUSTOM_OUTPUT_PATH_WARNING = `\
18	// ${yellow('Warning:')} You did not specify an output path for your \`generator\` in schema.prisma. \
19	// This behavior is deprecated and will no longer be supported in Prisma 7.0.0. To learn more \
20	// visit https://pris.ly/cli/output-path`
21	
22	export class PrismaClientJsGenerator implements Generator {
23	  readonly name = BuiltInProvider.PrismaClientJs
24	
25	  #shouldResolvePrismaClient: boolean
26	  #runtimePath?: string
27	  #cachedPrismaClientPath: string | undefined
28	
29	  constructor({ shouldResolvePrismaClient = true, runtimePath }: PrismaClientJsGeneratorOptions = {}) {
30	    this.#shouldResolvePrismaClient = shouldResolvePrismaClient
31	    this.#runtimePath = runtimePath
32	  }
33	
34	  async getManifest(config: GeneratorConfig): Promise<GeneratorManifest> {
35	    // TODO: warning disabled for now until we fixed issues around custom output paths - see ORM-976
36	    // if (!config.output) {
37	    //   console.warn(MISSING_CUSTOM_OUTPUT_PATH_WARNING)
38	    // }
39	
40	    // If `this.#shouldResolvePrismaClient` is true, which is normally the case,
41	    // we find the default output path by resolving the path to the
42	    // `@prisma/client` package. While we resolve the absolute path to the
43	    // package itself, the generator will rewrite it to replace
44	    // `.../@prisma/client` with `.../.prisma/client`, as long as
45	    // `config.isCustomOutput` is false.
46	    //
47	    // If a custom output path is provided, then the default output path doesn't
48	    // matter and we could use a static value here, but that unfortunately
49	    // wouldn't allow us to avoid resolving the client directory and would only
50	    // delay that until the `generate` method call because we copy the runtime
51	    // files if a custom output path is used, and we also copy the WASM modules
52	    // regardles of the output path. Not copying the runtime files is a breaking
53	    // change, so we can only completely get rid of this behaviour in the new
54	    // generator.
55	    //
56	    // If `this.#shouldResolvePrismaClient` is false, which is the case when
57	    // using the JSON-RPC compatibility adapter for Prisma Studio, we should use
58	    // a static value here.
59	    const defaultOutput = this.#shouldResolvePrismaClient ? await this.#getPrismaClientPath(config) : '.prisma/client'
60	
61	    return {
62	      defaultOutput,
63	      prettyName: 'Prisma Client',
64	      version: clientVersion,
65	      requiresEngines: [],
66	      requiresEngineVersion: enginesVersion,
67	    }
68	  }
69	
70	  async generate(options: GeneratorOptions): Promise<void> {
71	    const outputDir = parseEnvValue(options.generator.output!)
72	
73	    await generateClient({
74	      datamodel: options.datamodel,
75	      schemaPath: options.schemaPath,
76	      binaryPaths: options.binaryPaths!,
77	      datasources: options.datasources,
78	      outputDir,
79	      copyRuntime: Boolean(options.generator.config.copyRuntime),
80	      copyRuntimeSourceMaps: Boolean(process.env.PRISMA_COPY_RUNTIME_SOURCEMAPS),
81	      runtimeSourcePath: await this.#getRuntimePath(options.generator),
82	      dmmf: options.dmmf,
83	      generator: options.generator,
84	      engineVersion: options.version,
85	      clientVersion,
86	      activeProvider: options.datasources[0]?.activeProvider,
87	      typedSql: options.typedSql,
88	      compilerBuild: parseCompilerBuildFromUnknown(options.generator.config.compilerBuild),
89	    })
90	  }
91	
92	  async #getPrismaClientPath(config: GeneratorConfig): Promise<string> {
93	    if (this.#cachedPrismaClientPath) {
94	      return this.#cachedPrismaClientPath
95	    }
96	
97	    this.#cachedPrismaClientPath = await resolvePrismaClient(path.dirname(config.sourceFilePath))
98	    return this.#cachedPrismaClientPath
99	  }
100	
101	  async #getRuntimePath(config: GeneratorConfig): Promise<string> {
102	    if (this.#runtimePath) {
103	      return this.#runtimePath
104	    }
105	
106	    this.#runtimePath = path.join(await this.#getPrismaClientPath(config), 'runtime')
107	    return this.#runtimePath
108	  }
109	}
110	
111	function parseCompilerBuildFromUnknown(value: unknown): 'fast' | 'small' {
112	  if (value === undefined) {
113	    return 'fast'
114	  }
115	  if (value === 'small' || value === 'fast') {
116	    return value
117	  }
118	  throw new Error(`Invalid compiler build: ${JSON.stringify(value)}, expected one of: "fast", "small"`)
119	}

4	import { getTsconfig } from 'get-tsconfig'
5	import { bold, dim, green } from 'kleur/colors'
6	
7	import { version as clientVersion } from '../package.json'
8	import { inferImportFileExtension, parseGeneratedFileExtension, parseImportFileExtension } from './file-extensions'
9	import { generateClient } from './generateClient'
10	import { inferModuleFormat, parseModuleFormatFromUnknown } from './module-format'
11	import { parseRuntimeTargetFromUnknown, RuntimeTargetInternal } from './runtime-targets'
12	
13	const missingOutputErrorMessage = `An output path is required for the \`prisma-client\` generator. Please provide an output path in your schema file:
14	
15	${dim(`generator client {
16	  provider = "prisma-client"`)}
17	${green('  output   = "../src/generated"')}
18	${dim('}')}
19	
20	${bold('Note:')} the output path is relative to the schema directory.
21	`
22	
23	function getOutputPath(config: GeneratorConfig): string {
24	  if (!config.output) {
25	    throw new Error(missingOutputErrorMessage)
26	  }
27	  return parseEnvValue(config.output)
28	}
29	
30	export class PrismaClientTsGenerator implements Generator {
31	  readonly name = 'prisma-client-ts'
32	
33	  getManifest(config: GeneratorConfig): Promise<GeneratorManifest> {
34	    return Promise.resolve({
35	      defaultOutput: getOutputPath(config),
36	      prettyName: 'Prisma Client',
37	      version: clientVersion,
38	      requiresEngines: [],
39	      requiresEngineVersion: enginesVersion,
40	    })
41	  }
42	
43	  async generate(options: GeneratorOptions): Promise<void> {
44	    const { config } = options.generator
45	    const outputDir = getOutputPath(options.generator)
46	    const tsconfig = getTsconfig(outputDir)?.config
47	
48	    const target = config.runtime !== undefined ? parseRuntimeTargetFromUnknown(config.runtime) : 'nodejs'
49	
50	    const generatedFileExtension =
51	      config.generatedFileExtension !== undefined ? parseGeneratedFileExtension(config.generatedFileExtension) : 'ts'
52	
53	    const importFileExtension =
54	      config.importFileExtension !== undefined
55	        ? parseImportFileExtension(config.importFileExtension)
56	        : inferImportFileExtension({
57	            tsconfig,
58	            generatedFileExtension,
59	            target,
60	          })
61	
62	    const moduleFormat =
63	      config.moduleFormat !== undefined
64	        ? parseModuleFormatFromUnknown(config.moduleFormat)
65	        : inferModuleFormat({
66	            tsconfig,
67	            generatedFileExtension,
68	            importFileExtension,
69	          })
70	
71	    await generateClient({
72	      datamodel: options.datamodel,
73	      schemaPath: options.schemaPath,
74	      binaryPaths: options.binaryPaths!,
75	      datasources: options.datasources,
76	      outputDir,
77	      runtimeBase: '@prisma/client/runtime',
78	      dmmf: options.dmmf,
79	      generator: options.generator,
80	      engineVersion: options.version,
81	      clientVersion,
82	      activeProvider: options.datasources[0]?.activeProvider,
83	      typedSql: options.typedSql,
84	      target,
85	      generatedFileExtension,
86	      importFileExtension,
87	      moduleFormat,
88	      tsNoCheckPreamble: true, // Set to false only during internal tests
89	      compilerBuild: parseCompilerBuildFromUnknown(options.generator.config.compilerBuild, target),
90	    })
91	  }
92	}
93	
94	function parseCompilerBuildFromUnknown(value: unknown, target: RuntimeTargetInternal): 'fast' | 'small' {
95	  if (value === undefined) {
96	    // using the 'small' build for 'vercel-edge' target to fit within their free tier limits
97	    return target === 'vercel-edge' ? 'small' : 'fast'
98	  }
99	  if (value === 'small' || value === 'fast') {
100	    return value
101	  }
102	  throw new Error(`Invalid compiler build: ${JSON.stringify(value)}, expected one of: "fast", "small"`)
103	}

5	export interface GeneratorConfig {
6	  name: string

8	  isCustomOutput?: boolean
9	  provider: EnvValue
10	  config: {
11	    /** `output` is a reserved name and will only be available directly at `generator.output` */
12	    output?: never
13	    /** `provider` is a reserved name and will only be available directly at `generator.provider` */
14	    provider?: never
15	    /** `binaryTargets` is a reserved name and will only be available directly at `generator.binaryTargets` */
16	    binaryTargets?: never
17	    /** `previewFeatures` is a reserved name and will only be available directly at `generator.previewFeatures` */
18	    previewFeatures?: never
19	  } & {
20	    [key: string]: string | string[] | undefined
21	  }
22	  binaryTargets: BinaryTargetsEnvValue[]
23	  // TODO why is this not optional?
24	  previewFeatures: string[]
25	  sourceFilePath: string
26	}
27	
28	export interface EnvValue {
29	  fromEnvVar: null | string
30	  value: null | string
31	}
32	
33	export interface BinaryTargetsEnvValue {
34	  fromEnvVar: string | null
35	  value: string
36	  native?: boolean
37	}
38	
39	export type ConnectorType =
40	  | 'mysql'
41	  | 'mongodb'
42	  | 'sqlite'
43	  | 'postgresql'
44	  | 'postgres' // TODO: we could normalize postgres to postgresql this in engines to reduce the complexity?
45	  | 'prisma+postgres' // Note: used for Prisma Postgres, managed by PDP
46	  | 'sqlserver'
47	  | 'cockroachdb'
48	
49	export type ActiveConnectorType = Exclude<ConnectorType, 'postgres' | 'prisma+postgres'>
50	
51	export interface DataSource {
52	  name: string
53	  provider: ConnectorType
54	  // In Rust, this comes from `Connector::provider_name()`
55	  activeProvider: ActiveConnectorType
56	  schemas: string[] | []
57	  sourceFilePath: string
58	}
59	
60	export type BinaryPaths = {
61	  schemaEngine?: { [binaryTarget: string]: string } // key: target, value: path
62	}
63	
64	/** The options passed to the generator implementations */
65	export type GeneratorOptions = {
66	  generator: GeneratorConfig
67	  // TODO: what is otherGenerators for?
68	  otherGenerators: GeneratorConfig[]
69	  schemaPath: string
70	  dmmf: DMMF.Document
71	  datasources: DataSource[]
72	  // TODO deprecate datamodel & rename to schema?
73	  datamodel: string
74	  // TODO is it really always version hash? Feature is unclear.
75	  version: string // version hash
76	  binaryPaths?: BinaryPaths
77	  noHints?: boolean
78	  allowNoModels?: boolean
79	  typedSql?: SqlQueryOutput[]
80	}
81

84	export type GeneratorManifest = {

87	  denylists?: {
88	    models?: string[]
89	    fields?: string[]
90	  }
91	  requiresGenerators?: string[]
92	  requiresEngines?: EngineType[]

94	  requiresEngineVersion?: string
95	}

3	import { BuildOptions } from './build'

5	type AdapterEntry = {
6	  entry: string
7	  outfile: string
8	}
9	
10	export function createAdapterConfig(entries: AdapterEntry[]): BuildOptions[] {
11	  return entries.flatMap(({ entry, outfile }) => {
12	    const baseName = path.basename(outfile)
13	
14	    return [
15	      {
16	        name: `${baseName}-cjs`,
17	        format: 'cjs' as const,
18	        bundle: true,
19	        entryPoints: [entry],
20	        outfile,
21	        outExtension: { '.js': '.js' },
22	        emitTypes: true,
23	      },
24	      {
25	        name: `${baseName}-esm`,
26	        format: 'esm' as const,
27	        bundle: true,
28	        entryPoints: [entry],
29	        outfile,
30	        outExtension: { '.js': '.mjs' },
31	        emitTypes: true,
32	      },
33	    ]
34	  })
35	}
36	
37	export const adapterConfig = createAdapterConfig([{ entry: 'src/index.ts', outfile: 'dist/index' }])

40	  {
41	    name: 'cjs',
42	    format: 'cjs',

44	    outdir: 'dist',
