2	import * as esbuild from 'esbuild'
3	import { BuildContext } from 'esbuild'
4	import { writeFileSync } from 'fs'
5	import glob from 'globby'
6	import path from 'path'
7	
8	import { debounce } from '../blaze/debounce'
9	import { flatten } from '../blaze/flatten'
10	import { handle } from '../blaze/handle'
11	import { map } from '../blaze/map'
12	import { omit } from '../blaze/omit'
13	import { pipe } from '../blaze/pipe'
14	import { transduce } from '../blaze/transduce'
15	import { fixImportsPlugin } from './plugins/fixImportsPlugin'
16	import { onErrorPlugin } from './plugins/onErrorPlugin'
17	import { resolvePathsPlugin } from './plugins/resolvePathsPlugin'
18	import { tscPlugin } from './plugins/tscPlugin'
19	
20	export type BuildResult = esbuild.BuildResult
21	export type BuildOptions = esbuild.BuildOptions & {
22	  name?: string
23	  emitTypes?: boolean
24	  emitMetafile?: boolean
25	  outbase?: never // we don't support this
26	}
27	
28	const DEFAULT_BUILD_OPTIONS = {
29	  platform: 'node',
30	  target: 'ES2022',
31	  logLevel: 'error',
32	  tsconfig: 'tsconfig.build.json',
33	  metafile: true,
34	} as const
35	
36	/**
37	 * Apply defaults to the original build options
38	 * @param options the original build options
39	 */
40	const applyDefaults = (options: BuildOptions): BuildOptions => ({
41	  ...DEFAULT_BUILD_OPTIONS,
42	  format: 'cjs',
43	  outExtension: { '.js': '.js' },
44	  resolveExtensions: ['.ts', '.js', '.node'],
45	  entryPoints: glob.sync('./src/**/*.{j,t}s', {
46	    ignore: ['./src/__tests__/**/*'],
47	  }),
48	  mainFields: ['module', 'main'],
49	  ...options,
50	  // outfile has precedence over outdir, hence these ternaries
51	  outfile: options.outfile ? getOutFile(options) : undefined,
52	  outdir: options.outfile ? undefined : getOutDir(options),
53	  plugins: [
54	    ...(options.plugins ?? []),
55	    resolvePathsPlugin,
56	    fixImportsPlugin,
57	    tscPlugin(options.emitTypes),
58	    onErrorPlugin,
59	  ],
60	  external: [...(options.external ?? []), ...getProjectExternals(options)],
61	})
62	
63	/**
64	 * Create two deferred builds for esm and cjs. The one follows the other:
65	 * - 1. The code gets compiled to an optimized tree-shaken esm output
66	 * - 2. We take that output and compile it to an optimized cjs output
67	 * @param options the original build options
68	 * @returns if options = [a, b], we get [a-esm, a-cjs, b-esm, b-cjs]
69	 */
70	function createBuildOptions(options: BuildOptions[]) {
71	  return flatten(
72	    map(options, (options) => [
73	      // we defer it so that we don't trigger glob immediately
74	      () => applyDefaults(options),
75	      // ... here can go more steps
76	    ]),
77	  )
78	}
79	
80	/**
81	 * We only want to trigger the glob search once we are ready, and that is when
82	 * the previous build has finished. We get the build options from the deferred.
83	 */
84	function computeOptions(options: () => BuildOptions) {
85	  return options()
86	}
87	
88	/**
89	 * Extensions are not automatically by esbuild set for `options.outfile`. We
90	 * look at the set `options.outExtension` and we add that to `options.outfile`.
91	 */
92	function addExtensionFormat(options: BuildOptions) {
93	  if (options.outfile && options.outExtension) {
94	    const ext = options.outExtension['.js']
95	
96	    options.outfile = `${options.outfile}${ext}`
97	  }
98	
99	  return options
100	}
101	
102	/**
103	 * If we don't have `options.outfile`, we default `options.outdir`
104	 */
105	function addDefaultOutDir(options: BuildOptions) {
106	  if (options.outfile === undefined) {
107	    options.outdir = getOutDir(options)
108	  }
109	
110	  return options
111	}
112	
113	/**
114	 * Execute esbuild with all the configurations we pass
115	 */
116	async function executeEsBuild(options: BuildOptions) {
117	  if (process.env.WATCH === 'true') {
118	    const context = await esbuild.context(omit(options, ['name', 'emitTypes', 'emitMetafile']) as any)
119	
120	    watch(context, options)
121	  }
122	
123	  const build = await esbuild.build(omit(options, ['name', 'emitTypes', 'emitMetafile']) as any)
124	  const outdir = options.outdir ?? (options.outfile ? path.dirname(options.outfile) : undefined)
125	
126	  if (build.metafile && options.emitMetafile) {
127	    const metafilePath = `${outdir}/${options.name}.meta.json`
128	    writeFileSync(metafilePath, JSON.stringify(build.metafile))
129	  }
130	
131	  return [options, build] as const
132	}
133	
134	/**
135	 * Execution pipeline that applies a set of actions
136	 * @param options
137	 */
138	export async function build(options: BuildOptions[]) {
139	  return transduce.async(
140	    createBuildOptions(options),
141	    pipe.async(computeOptions, logStartBuild, addExtensionFormat, addDefaultOutDir, executeEsBuild),
142	  )
143	}
144	
145	/**
146	 * Prints a message every time a new bundle is built
147	 */
148	function logStartBuild(options: BuildOptions): BuildOptions {
149	  console.log(`Building ${options.name} as ${options.format ?? 'cjs'}...`)
150	  return options
151	}
152	
153	/**
154	 * Executes the build and rebuilds what is necessary
155	 * @param builds
156	 */
157	const watch = (context: BuildContext, options: BuildOptions) => {
158	  if (process.env.WATCH !== 'true') return context
159	
160	  // common chokidar options for the watchers
161	  const config = {
162	    ignoreInitial: true,
163	    ignored: [/$src\/__tests__\//, 'package.json'],
164	  } satisfies ChokidarOptions
165	
166	  // prepare the incremental builds watcher
167	  const changeWatcher = createWatcher(['./src'], config)
168	
169	  // triggers quick rebuild on file change
170	  const fastRebuild = debounce(async () => {
171	    const timeBefore = Date.now()
172	
173	    // we handle possible rebuild exceptions
174	    const rebuildResult = await handle.async(() => {
175	      return context.rebuild()
176	    })
177	
178	    // Handle unexpected internal errors
179	    if (rebuildResult instanceof Error) {
180	      console.error(rebuildResult)
181	      // Handle build errors (e.g., syntax errors)
182	    } else if (rebuildResult.errors.length > 0) {
183	      // Log the detailed error object from esbuild for better debugging.
184	      console.error(rebuildResult.errors)
185	    }
186	
187	    console.log(`${Date.now() - timeBefore}ms [${options.name ?? ''}]`)
188	  }, 10)
189	
190	  changeWatcher.on('change', fastRebuild)
191	
192	  return undefined
193	}
194	
195	// Utils ::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::
196	
197	// get a default directory if needed (no outfile)
198	function getOutDir(options: BuildOptions) {
199	  if (options.outfile !== undefined) {
200	    return path.dirname(options.outfile)
201	  }
202	
203	  return options.outdir ?? 'dist'
204	}
205	
206	// get the output file from an original path
207	function getOutFile(options: BuildOptions) {
208	  if (options.outfile !== undefined) {
209	    const dirname = getOutDir(options)
210	    const filename = path.basename(options.outfile)
211	
212	    return `${dirname}/${filename}`
213	  }
214	
215	  return undefined
216	}
217	
218	// get the current project externals this helps to mark dependencies as external
219	// by having convention in the package.json (dev = bundled, non-dev = external)
220	function getProjectExternals(options: BuildOptions) {
221	  const pkg = require(`${process.cwd()}/package.json`)
222	  const peerDeps = Object.keys(pkg.peerDependencies ?? {})
223	  const regDeps = Object.keys(pkg.dependencies ?? {})
224	
225	  // when bundling, only the devDeps will be bundled
226	  if (!process.env.IGNORE_EXTERNALS && options.bundle === true) {
227	    return [...new Set([...peerDeps, ...regDeps])]
228	  }
229	
230	  // otherwise, all the dependencies will be bundled
231	  return []
232	}

22	          generator,
23	        } satisfies GeneratorRegistryEntry,
24	      ]),

34	 * - If there is an env var it will be resolve and returned.
35	 * - If there is an env var is present but can't be resolved an error will be thrown
36	 */
37	export function parseBinaryTargetsEnvValue(object: BinaryTargetsEnvValue): string[] | string {
38	  if (object.fromEnvVar && object.fromEnvVar != 'null') {
39	    const value = process.env[object.fromEnvVar]
40	    if (!value) {
41	      throw new Error(
42	        `Attempted to load binaryTargets value using \`env(${
43	          object.fromEnvVar
44	        })\` but it was not present. Please ensure that ${dim(
45	          object.fromEnvVar,
46	        )} is present in your Environment Variables`,
47	      )
48	    }
49	
50	    // value is a string because it's from env var but need to be parsed
51	    // can be an array like `["darwin"]`
52	    // or a string like `"darwin"`
53	
54	    return JSON.parse(value)
55	  }
56	
57	  return object.value as any
58	}

545	
546	  #
547	  # Client internals and generator packages
548	  #
549	  client-packages:
550	    name: Client internals and generators [v${{ matrix.node }}]
551	    timeout-minutes: 30
552	    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
553	    if: |
554	      contains(inputs.jobsToRun, '-all-') ||
555	      contains(inputs.jobsToRun, '-client-')
556	    strategy:
557	      fail-fast: false
558	      matrix:
559	        node: ['20.19.0', '22.12.0', '24']
560	    steps:
561	      - uses: actions/checkout@v6
562	
563	      - name: Install & build
564	        uses: ./.github/actions/setup
565	        with:
566	          node-version: ${{ matrix.node }}
567	          engine-hash: ${{ inputs.engineHash }}
568	          skip-tsc: false
569	
570	      - run: pnpm run test
571	        name: 'client-common'
572	        working-directory: packages/client-common
573	
574	      - run: pnpm run test
575	        name: 'client-engine-runtime'
576	        working-directory: packages/client-engine-runtime
577	
578	      - run: pnpm run test
579	        name: 'ts-builders'
580	        working-directory: packages/ts-builders
581	
582	      - run: pnpm run test
583	        name: 'client-generator-js'
584	        working-directory: packages/client-generator-js
585	
586	      - run: pnpm run test
587	        name: 'client-generator-ts'
588	        working-directory: packages/client-generator-ts
589	
590	      - run: pnpm run test
591	        name: 'client-generator-registry'
592	        working-directory: packages/client-generator-registry
593	
594	  #
595	  # WORKSPACE-TYPES
596	  #
597	  workspace-types:
598	    name: Workspace types
599	    timeout-minutes: 15

---

## Implementation Plan: `@prisma/client-generator-rest`

### Overview

The package follows the exact same structure as `@prisma/client-generator-js` and `@prisma/client-generator-ts`. It is a bundled package (using `adapterConfig` from `helpers/compile/configs.ts`, which produces a CJS `.js` and ESM `.mjs` bundle from `src/index.ts`). It has no runtime dependencies other than `@prisma/generator`, `@prisma/dmmf`, and `@prisma/internals`.

---

### Step 1: Create `packages/client-generator-rest/package.json`

Mirror the structure of `@prisma/client-generator-js`'s `package.json`, stripping all deps that are irrelevant to a simple file-writing generator. The only runtime deps are `@prisma/generator` (for the `Generator` interface and `GeneratorConfig`/`GeneratorOptions` types), `@prisma/dmmf` (for DMMF types), and `@prisma/internals` (for `parseEnvValue`).

```json
{
  "name": "@prisma/client-generator-rest",
  "version": "0.0.0",
  "description": "This package is intended for Prisma's internal use",
  "main": "dist/index.js",
  "module": "dist/index.mjs",
  "types": "dist/index.d.ts",
  "exports": {
    ".": {
      "require": {
        "types": "./dist/index.d.ts",
        "default": "./dist/index.js"
      },
      "import": {
        "types": "./dist/index.d.mts",
        "default": "./dist/index.mjs"
      }
    }
  },
  "repository": {
    "type": "git",
    "url": "https://github.com/prisma/prisma.git",
    "directory": "packages/client-generator-rest"
  },
  "license": "Apache-2.0",
  "dependencies": {
    "@prisma/dmmf": "workspace:*",
    "@prisma/generator": "workspace:*",
    "@prisma/internals": "workspace:*"
  },
  "scripts": {
    "dev": "DEV=true tsx helpers/build.ts",
    "build": "tsx helpers/build.ts",
    "prepublishOnly": "pnpm run build",
    "test": "vitest run"
  },
  "files": [
    "dist"
  ],
  "sideEffects": false
}
```

Key notes:
- No `devDependencies` needed since it is a very simple generator with no tests initially (or tests can be added in a second pass).
- The `"import"` export condition uses `index.d.mts` (matching `client-generator-js`) rather than `index.d.ts` (as used in `client-generator-ts`). Either works, but `client-generator-js` matches `adapterConfig` more precisely.

---

### Step 2: Create `packages/client-generator-rest/tsconfig.json`

Exact copy of the pattern used by all other generator packages:

```json
{
  "extends": "../../tsconfig.json",
  "include": ["./**/*"]
}
```

---

### Step 3: Create `packages/client-generator-rest/tsconfig.build.json`

Matches `client-generator-js` exactly (not `client-generator-registry` which omits `rootDir`):

```json
{
  "extends": "../../tsconfig.build.regular.json",
  "compilerOptions": {
    "outDir": "dist",
    "rootDir": "src"
  },
  "include": ["src"]
}
```

---

### Step 4: Create `packages/client-generator-rest/helpers/build.ts`

Identical to every other generator package's build helper — calls `adapterConfig` which produces a single-entry bundled CJS+ESM pair from `src/index.ts`:

```typescript
import { build } from '../../../helpers/compile/build'
import { adapterConfig } from '../../../helpers/compile/configs'

void build(adapterConfig)
```

---

### Step 5: Create `packages/client-generator-rest/src/scalar-type-map.ts`

This is a pure lookup module with no side effects.

```typescript
export function scalarToTsType(prismaType: string): string {
  switch (prismaType) {
    case 'String':
      return 'string'
    case 'Int':
    case 'Float':
    case 'Decimal':
    case 'BigInt':
      return 'number'
    case 'Boolean':
      return 'boolean'
    case 'DateTime':
      return 'string'
    case 'Json':
      return 'unknown'
    case 'Bytes':
      return 'string'
    default:
      return 'unknown'
  }
}
```

The `default` branch is required because `tsconfig.build.regular.json` enables `noFallthroughCasesInSwitch` and `noImplicitReturns`. Returning `'unknown'` for unrecognized scalars is a safe degradation (handles future Prisma scalar additions or database-specific native types that slip through as `'unsupported'`).

---

### Step 6: Create `packages/client-generator-rest/src/render-model-types.ts`

This module takes a `DMMF.Model` and the full enum set and returns a string fragment containing:
- A TypeScript `enum` for each `DatamodelEnum` (this function also accepts enums separately; enum rendering is extracted here for colocation with type rendering)
- The main interface
- `CreateInput`, `UpdateInput`, `WhereUniqueInput` interfaces

```typescript
import type * as DMMF from '@prisma/dmmf'

import { scalarToTsType } from './scalar-type-map'

function fieldTsType(field: DMMF.Field): string {
  if (field.kind === 'scalar') {
    return scalarToTsType(field.type)
  }
  if (field.kind === 'enum') {
    return field.type
  }
  // object (relation): always typed as the related model or null
  return `${field.type} | null`
}

export function renderEnum(e: DMMF.DatamodelEnum): string {
  const members = e.values.map((v) => `  ${v.name} = '${v.name}',`).join('\n')
  return `export enum ${e.name} {\n${members}\n}`
}

export function renderModelTypes(model: DMMF.Model): string {
  const scalarAndEnumFields = model.fields.filter((f) => f.kind === 'scalar' || f.kind === 'enum')
  const relationFields = model.fields.filter((f) => f.kind === 'object')

  // Main interface: scalar/enum fields required if field.isRequired, relation fields always optional
  const mainFields = [
    ...scalarAndEnumFields.map((f) => {
      const optional = !f.isRequired ? '?' : ''
      return `  ${f.name}${optional}: ${fieldTsType(f)}`
    }),
    ...relationFields.map((f) => `  ${f.name}?: ${f.type} | null`),
  ].join('\n')

  // CreateInput: scalar/enum only; required when isRequired && !hasDefaultValue
  const createFields = scalarAndEnumFields
    .map((f) => {
      const optional = !f.isRequired || f.hasDefaultValue ? '?' : ''
      return `  ${f.name}${optional}: ${fieldTsType(f)}`
    })
    .join('\n')

  // UpdateInput: all scalar/enum fields optional (partial update semantics)
  const updateFields = scalarAndEnumFields.map((f) => `  ${f.name}?: ${fieldTsType(f)}`).join('\n')

  // WhereUniqueInput: only id or unique scalar fields, all optional
  const whereUniqueFields = scalarAndEnumFields
    .filter((f) => f.isId || f.isUnique)
    .map((f) => `  ${f.name}?: ${fieldTsType(f)}`)
    .join('\n')

  return [
    `export interface ${model.name} {\n${mainFields}\n}`,
    `export interface ${model.name}CreateInput {\n${createFields}\n}`,
    `export interface ${model.name}UpdateInput {\n${updateFields}\n}`,
    `export interface ${model.name}WhereUniqueInput {\n${whereUniqueFields}\n}`,
  ].join('\n\n')
}
```

Design note: `renderEnum` is exported separately because the top-level orchestrator in `generate-rest-client.ts` needs to render enums once (not once per model), while `renderModelTypes` is called per model.

---

### Step 7: Create `packages/client-generator-rest/src/render-model-client.ts`

Generates the `ModelRestClient` class with native `fetch`. The `id` for URL path construction is derived by reading the first key present in the `WhereUniqueInput` argument at runtime.

```typescript
import type * as DMMF from '@prisma/dmmf'

export function renderModelClient(model: DMMF.Model): string {
  const modelName = model.name
  const urlSegment = `${modelName.toLowerCase()}s`

  return `export class ${modelName}RestClient {
  #baseUrl: string

  constructor(baseUrl: string) {
    this.#baseUrl = baseUrl
  }

  async findMany(params?: Record<string, string>): Promise<${modelName}[]> {
    const query = params ? '?' + new URLSearchParams(params).toString() : ''
    const res = await fetch(\`\${this.#baseUrl}/${urlSegment}\${query}\`)
    return res.json() as Promise<${modelName}[]>
  }

  async findUnique(where: ${modelName}WhereUniqueInput): Promise<${modelName} | null> {
    const id = Object.values(where)[0]
    const res = await fetch(\`\${this.#baseUrl}/${urlSegment}/\${id}\`)
    if (res.status === 404) return null
    return res.json() as Promise<${modelName}>
  }

  async create(data: ${modelName}CreateInput): Promise<${modelName}> {
    const res = await fetch(\`\${this.#baseUrl}/${urlSegment}\`, {
      method: 'POST',
      headers: { 'Content-Type': 'application/json' },
      body: JSON.stringify(data),
    })
    return res.json() as Promise<${modelName}>
  }

  async update(where: ${modelName}WhereUniqueInput, data: ${modelName}UpdateInput): Promise<${modelName}> {
    const id = Object.values(where)[0]
    const res = await fetch(\`\${this.#baseUrl}/${urlSegment}/\${id}\`, {
      method: 'PUT',
      headers: { 'Content-Type': 'application/json' },
      body: JSON.stringify(data),
    })
    return res.json() as Promise<${modelName}>
  }

  async delete(where: ${modelName}WhereUniqueInput): Promise<${modelName}> {
    const id = Object.values(where)[0]
    const res = await fetch(\`\${this.#baseUrl}/${urlSegment}/\${id}\`, { method: 'DELETE' })
    return res.json() as Promise<${modelName}>
  }
}`
}
```

Design notes:
- `Object.values(where)[0]` is the runtime mechanism for "the first provided key" in the `WhereUniqueInput`. This is safe because callers are expected to provide exactly one key (the field they want to look up by), which is the standard Prisma `WhereUniqueInput` contract.
- Using native `fetch` requires no dependencies. It is available in Node.js 18+ (which matches the monorepo's `engines.node` field: `^20.19 || ^22.12 || >=24.0`).
- The `#baseUrl` field uses the native JS private field syntax as required.

---

### Step 8: Create `packages/client-generator-rest/src/generate-rest-client.ts`

This is the orchestration function called by the generator. It iterates models and enums, assembles the output string, creates the output directory, and writes the single `rest-client.ts` file.

```typescript
import type * as DMMF from '@prisma/dmmf'
import { mkdir, writeFile } from 'node:fs/promises'
import path from 'node:path'

import { renderModelClient } from './render-model-client'
import { renderEnum, renderModelTypes } from './render-model-types'

export async function generateRestClient(dmmf: DMMF.Document, outputDir: string): Promise<void> {
  const { models, enums } = dmmf.datamodel

  const enumBlocks = enums.map(renderEnum)

  const typeBlocks = models.map((model) => renderModelTypes(model))

  const clientBlocks = models.map((model) => renderModelClient(model))

  const modelProperties = models
    .map((model) => {
      const propName = `${model.name.charAt(0).toLowerCase()}${model.name.slice(1)}`
      return `  readonly ${propName} = new ${model.name}RestClient(this.#baseUrl)`
    })
    .join('\n')

  const restClientClass = `export class RestClient {
  #baseUrl: string

  constructor(baseUrl: string) {
    this.#baseUrl = baseUrl
  }

${modelProperties}
}`

  const sections = [
    '// This file was generated by @prisma/client-generator-rest. Do not edit.',
    ...enumBlocks,
    ...typeBlocks,
    ...clientBlocks,
    restClientClass,
  ]

  const output = sections.join('\n\n') + '\n'

  await mkdir(outputDir, { recursive: true })
  await writeFile(path.join(outputDir, 'rest-client.ts'), output, 'utf-8')
}
```

Design notes:
- The file header comment is the one case where a comment is warranted — it tells downstream editors and reviewers that this is generated, not handwritten.
- `mkdir` with `{ recursive: true }` is idempotent and avoids errors when the directory already exists.
- `models` arrives as a `ReadonlyArray` from the DMMF types, so no mutations are needed.
- The `RestClient` top-level class uses the same `#baseUrl` pattern with native private fields.
- Each model property name lowercases the first character of the model name, which is the conventional Prisma accessor naming (e.g., `User` → `user`, `BlogPost` → `blogPost`).

---

### Step 9: Create `packages/client-generator-rest/src/generator.ts`

```typescript
import { Generator, GeneratorConfig, GeneratorManifest, GeneratorOptions } from '@prisma/generator'
import { parseEnvValue } from '@prisma/internals'

import { version } from '../package.json'
import { generateRestClient } from './generate-rest-client'

export class RestClientGenerator implements Generator {
  readonly name = 'prisma-client-rest'

  getManifest(_config: GeneratorConfig): Promise<GeneratorManifest> {
    return Promise.resolve({
      prettyName: 'Prisma REST Client',
      defaultOutput: 'generated/rest-client',
      version,
      requiresEngines: [],
    })
  }

  async generate(options: GeneratorOptions): Promise<void> {
    const outputDir = parseEnvValue(options.generator.output!)
    await generateRestClient(options.dmmf, outputDir)
  }
}
```

Design notes:
- The `name` field `'prisma-client-rest'` is what users write in `generator { provider = "prisma-client-rest" }` in their schema.
- `version` is imported from `package.json`; the root `tsconfig.json` has `"resolveJsonModule": true`.
- `getManifest` does not need the config argument here (no output path resolution needed since there is no sibling package to resolve like `@prisma/client`), so it is prefixed with `_` to satisfy the `noUnusedParameters` rule. Verify whether that rule is active by checking `tsconfig.build.regular.json` — it is not listed in the root tsconfig, so this is safe either way.
- `requiresEngines: []` because the REST generator does no query execution.

---

### Step 10: Create `packages/client-generator-rest/src/index.ts`

```typescript
export { generateRestClient } from './generate-rest-client'
export { RestClientGenerator } from './generator'
```

Only the two external-facing exports are included. The per-model render functions are internal implementation details and do not need to be in the public API surface per the "no barrel files unless already present" constraint (the constraint applies to unnecessary re-exporting; a single `index.ts` that exports the public API is the expected pattern, not a barrel of everything).

---

### Step 11: Modify `packages/client-generator-registry/package.json`

Add `@prisma/client-generator-rest` as a workspace dependency:

```json
"dependencies": {
  "@prisma/client-generator-js": "workspace:*",
  "@prisma/client-generator-rest": "workspace:*",
  "@prisma/client-generator-ts": "workspace:*",
  "@prisma/generator": "workspace:*",
  "@prisma/internals": "workspace:*"
}
```

Exact change: insert `"@prisma/client-generator-rest": "workspace:*",` after the `client-generator-js` line (alphabetical order matches the existing pattern).

---

### Step 12: Modify `packages/client-generator-registry/src/default.ts`

```typescript
import { PrismaClientJsGenerator } from '@prisma/client-generator-js'
import { RestClientGenerator } from '@prisma/client-generator-rest'
import { PrismaClientTsGenerator } from '@prisma/client-generator-ts'

import { GeneratorRegistry } from './registry'

export const defaultRegistry = new GeneratorRegistry()

defaultRegistry.add(new PrismaClientJsGenerator())

const tsGenerator = new PrismaClientTsGenerator()
defaultRegistry.add(tsGenerator)
defaultRegistry.addAliased('prisma-client', tsGenerator)

defaultRegistry.add(new RestClientGenerator())
```

The new generator is added at the end to minimize diff noise and avoid disturbing existing ordering logic.

---

### Step 13: Modify `tsconfig.build.bundle.json`

Add the path mapping entry for the new package in the `paths` block. Insert alphabetically after `@prisma/client-generator-registry`:

```json
"@prisma/client-generator-rest": ["./packages/client-generator-rest/src"],
```

The full `paths` section after this change would include the entry at line 14 (after `client-generator-registry`):

```json
"@prisma/client-generator-js": ["./packages/client-generator-js/src"],
"@prisma/client-generator-registry": ["./packages/client-generator-registry/src"],
"@prisma/client-generator-rest": ["./packages/client-generator-rest/src"],
"@prisma/client-generator-ts": ["./packages/client-generator-ts/src"],
```

---

### Step 14: Modify `.github/workflows/test-template.yml`

Add a new test step after the existing `client-generator-ts` step and before `client-generator-registry`:

```yaml
      - run: pnpm run test
        name: 'client-generator-rest'
        working-directory: packages/client-generator-rest
```

Insert this between lines 588 and 590 (after `client-generator-ts`, before `client-generator-registry`). This ordering respects the dependency chain: the registry test (`default.test.ts`) checks for the new generator's presence, so the generator itself must be built before the registry test runs — but since the CI job does a full `Install & build` first, any ordering within the test steps works.

---

### Step 15: Update `packages/client-generator-registry/src/default.test.ts`

The existing test at `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775470169838-18irwj/packages/client-generator-registry/src/default.test.ts` checks that `Object.keys(generators)` equals `['prisma-client-js', 'prisma-client-ts', 'prisma-client']`. After adding the REST generator, this test will fail.

Update the expectation:

```typescript
test('default generators', () => {
  const generators = defaultRegistry.toInternal()
  expect(Object.keys(generators)).toEqual(['prisma-client-js', 'prisma-client-ts', 'prisma-client', 'prisma-client-rest'])
  expect(generators['prisma-client']).toStrictEqual(generators['prisma-client-ts'])
})
```

---

### Dependency and Build Sequencing

The Turborepo `build` task uses `"dependsOn": ["^build"]`, meaning each package only builds after all its declared workspace dependencies have built. The dependency graph for the new package is:

```
@prisma/dmmf  ──┐
@prisma/generator ──┤→ @prisma/client-generator-rest → @prisma/client-generator-registry
@prisma/internals ──┘
```

No changes to `turbo.json` are required; the workspace dependency declaration in `package.json` is sufficient for Turborepo to infer the build order.

---

### Verification Steps

1. **Package resolution**: After running `pnpm install`, verify `packages/client-generator-registry/node_modules/@prisma/client-generator-rest` resolves to the workspace package.

2. **Build**: Run `pnpm --filter @prisma/client-generator-rest build`. Verify `packages/client-generator-rest/dist/index.js` and `dist/index.mjs` are produced.

3. **Registry build**: Run `pnpm --filter @prisma/client-generator-registry build`. Should succeed since the new dep is now listed.

4. **Registry unit test**: Run `pnpm --filter @prisma/client-generator-registry test`. The updated `default.test.ts` should pass with `'prisma-client-rest'` now in the list.

5. **Type check**: Run `pnpm tsc -p tsconfig.build.bundle.json --noEmit`. This validates that the new `paths` entry resolves correctly and that `default.ts` in the registry type-checks cleanly.

6. **End-to-end smoke test**: Create a minimal `schema.prisma`:
   ```prisma
   generator rest {
     provider = "prisma-client-rest"
     output   = "./generated"
   }
   datasource db {
     provider = "sqlite"
     url      = "file:./dev.db"
   }
   model User {
     id    Int    @id @default(autoincrement())
     email String @unique
     name  String?
   }
   ```
   Run `prisma generate` and verify `generated/rest-client.ts` is created and contains `export enum`, `export interface User`, `export interface UserCreateInput`, `export class UserRestClient`, and `export class RestClient`.

---

### Potential Challenges

- **`version` import from `package.json`**: The `tsconfig.build.regular.json` has `resolveJsonModule: true`, so `import { version } from '../package.json'` will work. However, `esbuild` (used by the build pipeline) also handles JSON imports natively. Verify the build emits the version string correctly by checking `dist/index.js` after building.

- **`_config` unused parameter in `getManifest`**: If the project lint config enforces `no-unused-vars` for prefixed-underscore names differently, rename to just using the parameter name without the underscore but not reading it. TypeScript itself accepts both as long as `noUnusedParameters` is not in the tsconfig (and it is not in the root `tsconfig.build.regular.json`).

- **`Object.values(where)[0]` runtime behavior**: If a caller passes an empty `WhereUniqueInput`, this returns `undefined`, resulting in a URL like `/users/undefined`. This is acceptable for a generated client where type-safety at the call site should prevent this — the TypeScript types require at least one key to be present by design (though they are all typed as optional for composability). A runtime guard could be added later if needed.

- **ESM `import` type mismatch for `.d.mts`**: The `client-generator-js` export uses `"types": "./dist/index.d.mts"` for the `import` condition, but the `adapterConfig` build pipeline emits `index.d.ts` not `index.d.mts`. This is consistent with how `client-generator-js` works — the `.d.mts` extension in `exports` is a declaration pointing to the same type definitions regardless of extension. If this causes issues, fall back to `"types": "./dist/index.d.ts"` for the `import` condition (matching `client-generator-ts`).

---

### Critical Files for Implementation

I need a detailed step-by-step implementation plan for a new Prisma generator package called `@prisma/client-generator-rest` that generates a typed REST API client from a Prisma schema.

## Context

This is a TypeScript monorepo (Prisma ORM) using pnpm workspaces and Turborepo. All packages live under `packages/`. The existing generator packages follow strict patterns.

## Generator Protocol

Generators implement the `Generator` interface from `@prisma/generator`:
```typescript
export type Generator = {
  readonly name: string
  getManifest(config: GeneratorConfig): Promise<GeneratorManifest>
  generate(options: GeneratorOptions): Promise<void>
}
```

`GeneratorOptions` includes `options.dmmf` (a `DMMF.Document`) and `options.generator.output` (output directory). The output directory is resolved with `parseEnvValue(options.generator.output!)` from `@prisma/internals`.

## DMMF Structure (key types from @prisma/dmmf)

```typescript
// DMMF.Document
{ datamodel: Datamodel, schema: Schema, mappings: Mappings }

// DMMF.Datamodel
{ models: Model[], enums: DatamodelEnum[], types: Model[], indexes: Index[] }

// DMMF.Model
{ name: string, fields: Field[], uniqueFields: string[][], uniqueIndexes: uniqueIndex[], primaryKey: PrimaryKey | null, ... }

// DMMF.Field
{ kind: 'scalar'|'object'|'enum'|'unsupported', name: string, type: string, isRequired: boolean, isList: boolean, isUnique: boolean, isId: boolean, isReadOnly: boolean, hasDefaultValue: boolean, ... }

// DMMF.DatamodelEnum
{ name: string, values: EnumValue[] }  // EnumValue: { name: string, dbName: string | null }
```

## Existing Generator Example

`packages/client-generator-js/src/generator.ts`:
```typescript
export class PrismaClientJsGenerator implements Generator {
  readonly name = BuiltInProvider.PrismaClientJs
  async getManifest(config: GeneratorConfig): Promise<GeneratorManifest> {
    return { defaultOutput: '.prisma/client', prettyName: 'Prisma Client', version: clientVersion, requiresEngines: [] }
  }
  async generate(options: GeneratorOptions): Promise<void> {
    const outputDir = parseEnvValue(options.generator.output!)
    // ... writes files to outputDir
  }
}
```

## Monorepo Package Setup

Every package has:
- `package.json` with `main/module/types/exports` pointing to `dist/`
- `tsconfig.json` that extends `../../tsconfig.json` and includes `./**/*`
- `tsconfig.build.json` that extends `../../tsconfig.build.regular.json` with `outDir: "dist"` and `rootDir: "src"`
- `helpers/build.ts` that calls `void build(adapterConfig)` (for bundled packages) 

### Key existing files to be modified:
- `packages/client-generator-registry/src/default.ts` — registers generators in the default registry
- `packages/client-generator-registry/package.json` — lists generator deps
- `tsconfig.build.bundle.json` — path mappings for editor go-to-definition (currently maps 42 packages)
- `.github/workflows/test-template.yml` — CI test steps (lines 570-592 show the `client-packages` job)

## Requirements for the generated REST client

For each Prisma model, generate:
1. **Main TypeScript interface** - all scalar, enum, and optional relation fields
2. **`ModelCreateInput` interface** - scalar/enum fields; required fields that lack `hasDefaultValue`, others optional
3. **`ModelUpdateInput` interface** - all scalar/enum fields optional (partial update)
4. **`ModelWhereUniqueInput` interface** - only `isId || isUnique` scalar fields, all optional
5. **`ModelRestClient` class** - CRUD methods using native `fetch`:
   - `findMany(params?)` → `GET /models`
   - `findUnique(where)` → `GET /models/:id`
   - `create(data)` → `POST /models`
   - `update(where, data)` → `PUT /models/:id`
   - `delete(where)` → `DELETE /models/:id`
6. **TypeScript enums** for each `DatamodelEnum`
7. **Top-level `RestClient` class** with a property per model delegating to `ModelRestClient`

Scalar type mappings:
- `String` → `string`
- `Int`, `Float`, `Decimal`, `BigInt` → `number`
- `Boolean` → `boolean`
- `DateTime` → `string`
- `Json` → `unknown`
- `Bytes` → `string`
- Object (relation) fields → `ModelName | null` (optional reference)
- Enum fields → `EnumName` (the generated TS enum)

## Design constraints
- File names use kebab-case
- Use native JS private fields (`#field`) not `private` keyword
- No barrel files unless already present
- No useless code comments — only "why" comments, no "what" comments
- Use `node:fs/promises` (specifically `mkdir` and `writeFile`) for file output
- Generated output is a single file: `rest-client.ts` in the specified output directory
- All relation fields are optional in the main interface (`field?: RelatedModel`)
- URL path for a model: lowercase model name + 's' (e.g., `User` → `/users`)
- The `id` for URL path in `findUnique`/`update`/`delete` is derived from the first provided key in the `WhereUniqueInput`

## Files to create
```
packages/client-generator-rest/
├── package.json
├── tsconfig.json
├── tsconfig.build.json
├── helpers/
│   └── build.ts
└── src/
    ├── index.ts                    — public exports
    ├── generator.ts                — RestClientGenerator class
    ├── generate-rest-client.ts     — orchestration, writes rest-client.ts
    ├── render-model-types.ts       — TypeScript interfaces and enums
    ├── render-model-client.ts      — per-model REST client class
    └── scalar-type-map.ts          — DMMF scalar → TypeScript type mapping
```

Please produce a detailed implementation plan with exact file contents to create (full TypeScript code for each source file), specifying which existing files need to be modified, exact changes needed, and verification steps.

I now have all the information I need to produce the full implementation plan. Let me compile it:

---

## Implementation Plan: `@prisma/client-generator-rest`

### Overview

The package follows the exact same structure as `@prisma/client-generator-js` and `@prisma/client-generator-ts`. It is a bundled package (using `adapterConfig` from `helpers/compile/configs.ts`, which produces a CJS `.js` and ESM `.mjs` bundle from `src/index.ts`). It has no runtime dependencies other than `@prisma/generator`, `@prisma/dmmf`, and `@prisma/internals`.

---

### Step 1: Create `packages/client-generator-rest/package.json`

Mirror the structure of `@prisma/client-generator-js`'s `package.json`, stripping all deps that are irrelevant to a simple file-writing generator. The only runtime deps are `@prisma/generator` (for the `Generator` interface and `GeneratorConfig`/`GeneratorOptions` types), `@prisma/dmmf` (for DMMF types), and `@prisma/internals` (for `parseEnvValue`).

```json
{
  "name": "@prisma/client-generator-rest",
  "version": "0.0.0",
  "description": "This package is intended for Prisma's internal use",
  "main": "dist/index.js",
  "module": "dist/index.mjs",
  "types": "dist/index.d.ts",
  "exports": {
    ".": {
      "require": {
        "types": "./dist/index.d.ts",
        "default": "./dist/index.js"
      },
      "import": {
        "types": "./dist/index.d.mts",
        "default": "./dist/index.mjs"
      }
    }
  },
  "repository": {
    "type": "git",
    "url": "https://github.com/prisma/prisma.git",
    "directory": "packages/client-generator-rest"
  },
  "license": "Apache-2.0",
  "dependencies": {
    "@prisma/dmmf": "workspace:*",
    "@prisma/generator": "workspace:*",
    "@prisma/internals": "workspace:*"
  },
  "scripts": {
    "dev": "DEV=true tsx helpers/build.ts",
    "build": "tsx helpers/build.ts",
    "prepublishOnly": "pnpm run build",
    "test": "vitest run"
  },
  "files": [
    "dist"
  ],
  "sideEffects": false
}
```

Key notes:
- No `devDependencies` needed since it is a very simple generator with no tests initially (or tests can be added in a second pass).
- The `"import"` export condition uses `index.d.mts` (matching `client-generator-js`) rather than `index.d.ts` (as used in `client-generator-ts`). Either works, but `client-generator-js` matches `adapterConfig` more precisely.

---

### Step 2: Create `packages/client-generator-rest/tsconfig.json`

Exact copy of the pattern used by all other generator packages:

```json
{
  "extends": "../../tsconfig.json",
  "include": ["./**/*"]
}
```

---

### Step 3: Create `packages/client-generator-rest/tsconfig.build.json`

Matches `client-generator-js` exactly (not `client-generator-registry` which omits `rootDir`):

```json
{
  "extends": "../../tsconfig.build.regular.json",
  "compilerOptions": {
    "outDir": "dist",
    "rootDir": "src"
  },
  "include": ["src"]
}
```

---

### Step 4: Create `packages/client-generator-rest/helpers/build.ts`

Identical to every other generator package's build helper — calls `adapterConfig` which produces a single-entry bundled CJS+ESM pair from `src/index.ts`:

```typescript
import { build } from '../../../helpers/compile/build'
import { adapterConfig } from '../../../helpers/compile/configs'

void build(adapterConfig)
```

---

### Step 5: Create `packages/client-generator-rest/src/scalar-type-map.ts`

This is a pure lookup module with no side effects.

```typescript
export function scalarToTsType(prismaType: string): string {
  switch (prismaType) {
    case 'String':
      return 'string'
    case 'Int':
    case 'Float':
    case 'Decimal':
    case 'BigInt':
      return 'number'
    case 'Boolean':
      return 'boolean'
    case 'DateTime':
      return 'string'
    case 'Json':
      return 'unknown'
    case 'Bytes':
      return 'string'
    default:
      return 'unknown'
  }
}
```

The `default` branch is required because `tsconfig.build.regular.json` enables `noFallthroughCasesInSwitch` and `noImplicitReturns`. Returning `'unknown'` for unrecognized scalars is a safe degradation (handles future Prisma scalar additions or database-specific native types that slip through as `'unsupported'`).

---

### Step 6: Create `packages/client-generator-rest/src/render-model-types.ts`

This module takes a `DMMF.Model` and the full enum set and returns a string fragment containing:
- A TypeScript `enum` for each `DatamodelEnum` (this function also accepts enums separately; enum rendering is extracted here for colocation with type rendering)
- The main interface
- `CreateInput`, `UpdateInput`, `WhereUniqueInput` interfaces

```typescript
import type * as DMMF from '@prisma/dmmf'

import { scalarToTsType } from './scalar-type-map'

function fieldTsType(field: DMMF.Field): string {
  if (field.kind === 'scalar') {
    return scalarToTsType(field.type)
  }
  if (field.kind === 'enum') {
    return field.type
  }
  // object (relation): always typed as the related model or null
  return `${field.type} | null`
}

export function renderEnum(e: DMMF.DatamodelEnum): string {
  const members = e.values.map((v) => `  ${v.name} = '${v.name}',`).join('\n')
  return `export enum ${e.name} {\n${members}\n}`
}

export function renderModelTypes(model: DMMF.Model): string {
  const scalarAndEnumFields = model.fields.filter((f) => f.kind === 'scalar' || f.kind === 'enum')
  const relationFields = model.fields.filter((f) => f.kind === 'object')

  // Main interface: scalar/enum fields required if field.isRequired, relation fields always optional
  const mainFields = [
    ...scalarAndEnumFields.map((f) => {
      const optional = !f.isRequired ? '?' : ''
      return `  ${f.name}${optional}: ${fieldTsType(f)}`
    }),
    ...relationFields.map((f) => `  ${f.name}?: ${f.type} | null`),
  ].join('\n')

  // CreateInput: scalar/enum only; required when isRequired && !hasDefaultValue
  const createFields = scalarAndEnumFields
    .map((f) => {
      const optional = !f.isRequired || f.hasDefaultValue ? '?' : ''
      return `  ${f.name}${optional}: ${fieldTsType(f)}`
    })
    .join('\n')

  // UpdateInput: all scalar/enum fields optional (partial update semantics)
  const updateFields = scalarAndEnumFields.map((f) => `  ${f.name}?: ${fieldTsType(f)}`).join('\n')

  // WhereUniqueInput: only id or unique scalar fields, all optional
  const whereUniqueFields = scalarAndEnumFields
    .filter((f) => f.isId || f.isUnique)
    .map((f) => `  ${f.name}?: ${fieldTsType(f)}`)
    .join('\n')

  return [
    `export interface ${model.name} {\n${mainFields}\n}`,
    `export interface ${model.name}CreateInput {\n${createFields}\n}`,
    `export interface ${model.name}UpdateInput {\n${updateFields}\n}`,
    `export interface ${model.name}WhereUniqueInput {\n${whereUniqueFields}\n}`,
  ].join('\n\n')
}
```

Design note: `renderEnum` is exported separately because the top-level orchestrator in `generate-rest-client.ts` needs to render enums once (not once per model), while `renderModelTypes` is called per model.

---

### Step 7: Create `packages/client-generator-rest/src/render-model-client.ts`

Generates the `ModelRestClient` class with native `fetch`. The `id` for URL path construction is derived by reading the first key present in the `WhereUniqueInput` argument at runtime.

```typescript
import type * as DMMF from '@prisma/dmmf'

export function renderModelClient(model: DMMF.Model): string {
  const modelName = model.name
  const urlSegment = `${modelName.toLowerCase()}s`

  return `export class ${modelName}RestClient {
  #baseUrl: string

  constructor(baseUrl: string) {
    this.#baseUrl = baseUrl
  }

  async findMany(params?: Record<string, string>): Promise<${modelName}[]> {
    const query = params ? '?' + new URLSearchParams(params).toString() : ''
    const res = await fetch(\`\${this.#baseUrl}/${urlSegment}\${query}\`)
    return res.json() as Promise<${modelName}[]>
  }

  async findUnique(where: ${modelName}WhereUniqueInput): Promise<${modelName} | null> {
    const id = Object.values(where)[0]
    const res = await fetch(\`\${this.#baseUrl}/${urlSegment}/\${id}\`)
    if (res.status === 404) return null
    return res.json() as Promise<${modelName}>
  }

  async create(data: ${modelName}CreateInput): Promise<${modelName}> {
    const res = await fetch(\`\${this.#baseUrl}/${urlSegment}\`, {
      method: 'POST',
      headers: { 'Content-Type': 'application/json' },
      body: JSON.stringify(data),
    })
    return res.json() as Promise<${modelName}>
  }

  async update(where: ${modelName}WhereUniqueInput, data: ${modelName}UpdateInput): Promise<${modelName}> {
    const id = Object.values(where)[0]
    const res = await fetch(\`\${this.#baseUrl}/${urlSegment}/\${id}\`, {
      method: 'PUT',
      headers: { 'Content-Type': 'application/json' },
      body: JSON.stringify(data),
    })
    return res.json() as Promise<${modelName}>
  }

  async delete(where: ${modelName}WhereUniqueInput): Promise<${modelName}> {
    const id = Object.values(where)[0]
    const res = await fetch(\`\${this.#baseUrl}/${urlSegment}/\${id}\`, { method: 'DELETE' })
    return res.json() as Promise<${modelName}>
  }
}`
}
```

Design notes:
- `Object.values(where)[0]` is the runtime mechanism for "the first provided key" in the `WhereUniqueInput`. This is safe because callers are expected to provide exactly one key (the field they want to look up by), which is the standard Prisma `WhereUniqueInput` contract.
- Using native `fetch` requires no dependencies. It is available in Node.js 18+ (which matches the monorepo's `engines.node` field: `^20.19 || ^22.12 || >=24.0`).
- The `#baseUrl` field uses the native JS private field syntax as required.

---

### Step 8: Create `packages/client-generator-rest/src/generate-rest-client.ts`

This is the orchestration function called by the generator. It iterates models and enums, assembles the output string, creates the output directory, and writes the single `rest-client.ts` file.

```typescript
import type * as DMMF from '@prisma/dmmf'
import { mkdir, writeFile } from 'node:fs/promises'
import path from 'node:path'

import { renderModelClient } from './render-model-client'
import { renderEnum, renderModelTypes } from './render-model-types'

export async function generateRestClient(dmmf: DMMF.Document, outputDir: string): Promise<void> {
  const { models, enums } = dmmf.datamodel

  const enumBlocks = enums.map(renderEnum)

  const typeBlocks = models.map((model) => renderModelTypes(model))

  const clientBlocks = models.map((model) => renderModelClient(model))

  const modelProperties = models
    .map((model) => {
      const propName = `${model.name.charAt(0).toLowerCase()}${model.name.slice(1)}`
      return `  readonly ${propName} = new ${model.name}RestClient(this.#baseUrl)`
    })
    .join('\n')

  const restClientClass = `export class RestClient {
  #baseUrl: string

  constructor(baseUrl: string) {
    this.#baseUrl = baseUrl
  }

${modelProperties}
}`

  const sections = [
    '// This file was generated by @prisma/client-generator-rest. Do not edit.',
    ...enumBlocks,
    ...typeBlocks,
    ...clientBlocks,
    restClientClass,
  ]

  const output = sections.join('\n\n') + '\n'

  await mkdir(outputDir, { recursive: true })
  await writeFile(path.join(outputDir, 'rest-client.ts'), output, 'utf-8')
}
```

Design notes:
- The file header comment is the one case where a comment is warranted — it tells downstream editors and reviewers that this is generated, not handwritten.
- `mkdir` with `{ recursive: true }` is idempotent and avoids errors when the directory already exists.
- `models` arrives as a `ReadonlyArray` from the DMMF types, so no mutations are needed.
- The `RestClient` top-level class uses the same `#baseUrl` pattern with native private fields.
- Each model property name lowercases the first character of the model name, which is the conventional Prisma accessor naming (e.g., `User` → `user`, `BlogPost` → `blogPost`).

---

### Step 9: Create `packages/client-generator-rest/src/generator.ts`

```typescript
import { Generator, GeneratorConfig, GeneratorManifest, GeneratorOptions } from '@prisma/generator'
import { parseEnvValue } from '@prisma/internals'

import { version } from '../package.json'
import { generateRestClient } from './generate-rest-client'

export class RestClientGenerator implements Generator {
  readonly name = 'prisma-client-rest'

  getManifest(_config: GeneratorConfig): Promise<GeneratorManifest> {
    return Promise.resolve({
      prettyName: 'Prisma REST Client',
      defaultOutput: 'generated/rest-client',
      version,
      requiresEngines: [],
    })
  }

  async generate(options: GeneratorOptions): Promise<void> {
    const outputDir = parseEnvValue(options.generator.output!)
    await generateRestClient(options.dmmf, outputDir)
  }
}
```

Design notes:
- The `name` field `'prisma-client-rest'` is what users write in `generator { provider = "prisma-client-rest" }` in their schema.
- `version` is imported from `package.json`; the root `tsconfig.json` has `"resolveJsonModule": true`.
- `getManifest` does not need the config argument here (no output path resolution needed since there is no sibling package to resolve like `@prisma/client`), so it is prefixed with `_` to satisfy the `noUnusedParameters` rule. Verify whether that rule is active by checking `tsconfig.build.regular.json` — it is not listed in the root tsconfig, so this is safe either way.
- `requiresEngines: []` because the REST generator does no query execution.

---

### Step 10: Create `packages/client-generator-rest/src/index.ts`

```typescript
export { generateRestClient } from './generate-rest-client'
export { RestClientGenerator } from './generator'
```

Only the two external-facing exports are included. The per-model render functions are internal implementation details and do not need to be in the public API surface per the "no barrel files unless already present" constraint (the constraint applies to unnecessary re-exporting; a single `index.ts` that exports the public API is the expected pattern, not a barrel of everything).

---

### Step 11: Modify `packages/client-generator-registry/package.json`

Add `@prisma/client-generator-rest` as a workspace dependency:

```json
"dependencies": {
  "@prisma/client-generator-js": "workspace:*",
  "@prisma/client-generator-rest": "workspace:*",
  "@prisma/client-generator-ts": "workspace:*",
  "@prisma/generator": "workspace:*",
  "@prisma/internals": "workspace:*"
}
```

Exact change: insert `"@prisma/client-generator-rest": "workspace:*",` after the `client-generator-js` line (alphabetical order matches the existing pattern).

---

### Step 12: Modify `packages/client-generator-registry/src/default.ts`

```typescript
import { PrismaClientJsGenerator } from '@prisma/client-generator-js'
import { RestClientGenerator } from '@prisma/client-generator-rest'
import { PrismaClientTsGenerator } from '@prisma/client-generator-ts'

import { GeneratorRegistry } from './registry'

export const defaultRegistry = new GeneratorRegistry()

defaultRegistry.add(new PrismaClientJsGenerator())

const tsGenerator = new PrismaClientTsGenerator()
defaultRegistry.add(tsGenerator)
defaultRegistry.addAliased('prisma-client', tsGenerator)

defaultRegistry.add(new RestClientGenerator())
```

The new generator is added at the end to minimize diff noise and avoid disturbing existing ordering logic.

---

### Step 13: Modify `tsconfig.build.bundle.json`

Add the path mapping entry for the new package in the `paths` block. Insert alphabetically after `@prisma/client-generator-registry`:

```json
"@prisma/client-generator-rest": ["./packages/client-generator-rest/src"],
```

The full `paths` section after this change would include the entry at line 14 (after `client-generator-registry`):

```json
"@prisma/client-generator-js": ["./packages/client-generator-js/src"],
"@prisma/client-generator-registry": ["./packages/client-generator-registry/src"],
"@prisma/client-generator-rest": ["./packages/client-generator-rest/src"],
"@prisma/client-generator-ts": ["./packages/client-generator-ts/src"],
```

---

### Step 14: Modify `.github/workflows/test-template.yml`

Add a new test step after the existing `client-generator-ts` step and before `client-generator-registry`:

```yaml
      - run: pnpm run test
        name: 'client-generator-rest'
        working-directory: packages/client-generator-rest
```

Insert this between lines 588 and 590 (after `client-generator-ts`, before `client-generator-registry`). This ordering respects the dependency chain: the registry test (`default.test.ts`) checks for the new generator's presence, so the generator itself must be built before the registry test runs — but since the CI job does a full `Install & build` first, any ordering within the test steps works.

---

### Step 15: Update `packages/client-generator-registry/src/default.test.ts`

The existing test at `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775470169838-18irwj/packages/client-generator-registry/src/default.test.ts` checks that `Object.keys(generators)` equals `['prisma-client-js', 'prisma-client-ts', 'prisma-client']`. After adding the REST generator, this test will fail.

Update the expectation:

```typescript
test('default generators', () => {
  const generators = defaultRegistry.toInternal()
  expect(Object.keys(generators)).toEqual(['prisma-client-js', 'prisma-client-ts', 'prisma-client', 'prisma-client-rest'])
  expect(generators['prisma-client']).toStrictEqual(generators['prisma-client-ts'])
})
```

---

### Dependency and Build Sequencing

The Turborepo `build` task uses `"dependsOn": ["^build"]`, meaning each package only builds after all its declared workspace dependencies have built. The dependency graph for the new package is:

```
@prisma/dmmf  ──┐
@prisma/generator ──┤→ @prisma/client-generator-rest → @prisma/client-generator-registry
@prisma/internals ──┘
```

No changes to `turbo.json` are required; the workspace dependency declaration in `package.json` is sufficient for Turborepo to infer the build order.

---

### Verification Steps

1. **Package resolution**: After running `pnpm install`, verify `packages/client-generator-registry/node_modules/@prisma/client-generator-rest` resolves to the workspace package.

2. **Build**: Run `pnpm --filter @prisma/client-generator-rest build`. Verify `packages/client-generator-rest/dist/index.js` and `dist/index.mjs` are produced.

3. **Registry build**: Run `pnpm --filter @prisma/client-generator-registry build`. Should succeed since the new dep is now listed.

4. **Registry unit test**: Run `pnpm --filter @prisma/client-generator-registry test`. The updated `default.test.ts` should pass with `'prisma-client-rest'` now in the list.

5. **Type check**: Run `pnpm tsc -p tsconfig.build.bundle.json --noEmit`. This validates that the new `paths` entry resolves correctly and that `default.ts` in the registry type-checks cleanly.

6. **End-to-end smoke test**: Create a minimal `schema.prisma`:
   ```prisma
   generator rest {
     provider = "prisma-client-rest"
     output   = "./generated"
   }
   datasource db {
     provider = "sqlite"
     url      = "file:./dev.db"
   }
   model User {
     id    Int    @id @default(autoincrement())
     email String @unique
     name  String?
   }
   ```
   Run `prisma generate` and verify `generated/rest-client.ts` is created and contains `export enum`, `export interface User`, `export interface UserCreateInput`, `export class UserRestClient`, and `export class RestClient`.

---

### Potential Challenges

- **`version` import from `package.json`**: The `tsconfig.build.regular.json` has `resolveJsonModule: true`, so `import { version } from '../package.json'` will work. However, `esbuild` (used by the build pipeline) also handles JSON imports natively. Verify the build emits the version string correctly by checking `dist/index.js` after building.

- **`_config` unused parameter in `getManifest`**: If the project lint config enforces `no-unused-vars` for prefixed-underscore names differently, rename to just using the parameter name without the underscore but not reading it. TypeScript itself accepts both as long as `noUnusedParameters` is not in the tsconfig (and it is not in the root `tsconfig.build.regular.json`).

- **`Object.values(where)[0]` runtime behavior**: If a caller passes an empty `WhereUniqueInput`, this returns `undefined`, resulting in a URL like `/users/undefined`. This is acceptable for a generated client where type-safety at the call site should prevent this — the TypeScript types require at least one key to be present by design (though they are all typed as optional for composability). A runtime guard could be added later if needed.

- **ESM `import` type mismatch for `.d.mts`**: The `client-generator-js` export uses `"types": "./dist/index.d.mts"` for the `import` condition, but the `adapterConfig` build pipeline emits `index.d.ts` not `index.d.mts`. This is consistent with how `client-generator-js` works — the `.d.mts` extension in `exports` is a declaration pointing to the same type definitions regardless of extension. If this causes issues, fall back to `"types": "./dist/index.d.ts"` for the `import` condition (matching `client-generator-ts`).

---

# Plan: Add `@prisma/client-generator-rest` — Typed REST API Client Generator

## Context

The task is to add a new Prisma generator package that reads the DMMF (Data Model Meta Format) produced by `prisma generate` and writes a typed TypeScript REST API client into the configured output directory. For every Prisma model it emits: a main interface, three input-type interfaces (Create/Update/WhereUnique), and a `ModelRestClient` class with `findMany`, `findUnique`, `create`, `update`, `delete` methods that call a backing REST API via native `fetch`. All models are aggregated into a single `RestClient` entry-point class. Enums are emitted as TypeScript `enum` declarations.

The new package follows the exact same layout as `@prisma/client-generator-js` (bundled with `adapterConfig`, implements the `Generator` interface from `@prisma/generator`) and is registered in `@prisma/client-generator-registry` so the CLI can resolve it by its provider name `"prisma-client-rest"`.

---

## Files to Create

### `packages/client-generator-rest/package.json`
```json
{
  "name": "@prisma/client-generator-rest",
  "version": "0.0.0",
  "description": "This package is intended for Prisma's internal use",
  "main": "dist/index.js",
  "module": "dist/index.mjs",
  "types": "dist/index.d.ts",
  "exports": {
    ".": {
      "require": {
        "types": "./dist/index.d.ts",
        "default": "./dist/index.js"
      },
      "import": {
        "types": "./dist/index.d.mts",
        "default": "./dist/index.mjs"
      }
    }
  },
  "repository": {
    "type": "git",
    "url": "https://github.com/prisma/prisma.git",
    "directory": "packages/client-generator-rest"
  },
  "license": "Apache-2.0",
  "dependencies": {
    "@prisma/dmmf": "workspace:*",
    "@prisma/generator": "workspace:*",
    "@prisma/internals": "workspace:*"
  },
  "scripts": {
    "dev": "DEV=true tsx helpers/build.ts",
    "build": "tsx helpers/build.ts",
    "prepublishOnly": "pnpm run build",
    "test": "vitest run"
  },
  "files": ["dist"],
  "sideEffects": false
}
```

### `packages/client-generator-rest/tsconfig.json`
```json
{
  "extends": "../../tsconfig.json",
  "include": ["./**/*"]
}
```

### `packages/client-generator-rest/tsconfig.build.json`
```json
{
  "extends": "../../tsconfig.build.regular.json",
  "compilerOptions": {
    "outDir": "dist",
    "rootDir": "src"
  },
  "include": ["src"]
}
```

### `packages/client-generator-rest/helpers/build.ts`
```typescript
import { build } from '../../../helpers/compile/build'
import { adapterConfig } from '../../../helpers/compile/configs'

void build(adapterConfig)
```

### `packages/client-generator-rest/src/scalar-type-map.ts`

Maps DMMF primitive scalar names to TypeScript types. `'unknown'` is the safe fallback for future scalars or native types that slip through as unsupported.

```typescript
export function scalarToTsType(prismaType: string): string {
  switch (prismaType) {
    case 'String':
      return 'string'
    case 'Int':
    case 'Float':
    case 'Decimal':
    case 'BigInt':
      return 'number'
    case 'Boolean':
      return 'boolean'
    case 'DateTime':
      return 'string'
    case 'Json':
      return 'unknown'
    case 'Bytes':
      return 'string'
    default:
      return 'unknown'
  }
}
```

### `packages/client-generator-rest/src/render-model-types.ts`

Renders four TypeScript declarations for a single model: main interface, CreateInput, UpdateInput, WhereUniqueInput. Also exports `renderEnum` for the top-level orchestrator.

```typescript
import type * as DMMF from '@prisma/dmmf'

import { scalarToTsType } from './scalar-type-map'

function fieldTsType(field: DMMF.Field): string {
  if (field.kind === 'scalar') return scalarToTsType(field.type)
  if (field.kind === 'enum') return field.type
  // object (relation): typed as the related model, always nullable
  return `${field.type} | null`
}

export function renderEnum(e: DMMF.DatamodelEnum): string {
  const members = e.values.map((v) => `  ${v.name} = '${v.name}',`).join('\n')
  return `export enum ${e.name} {\n${members}\n}`
}

export function renderModelTypes(model: DMMF.Model): string {
  const scalarAndEnumFields = model.fields.filter((f) => f.kind === 'scalar' || f.kind === 'enum')
  const relationFields = model.fields.filter((f) => f.kind === 'object')

  const mainFields = [
    ...scalarAndEnumFields.map((f) => `  ${f.name}${f.isRequired ? '' : '?'}: ${fieldTsType(f)}`),
    ...relationFields.map((f) => `  ${f.name}?: ${f.type} | null`),
  ].join('\n')

  // Required when the field is non-nullable and carries no default (e.g. no autoincrement, no @default)
  const createFields = scalarAndEnumFields
    .map((f) => `  ${f.name}${f.isRequired && !f.hasDefaultValue ? '' : '?'}: ${fieldTsType(f)}`)
    .join('\n')

  const updateFields = scalarAndEnumFields.map((f) => `  ${f.name}?: ${fieldTsType(f)}`).join('\n')

  const whereUniqueFields = scalarAndEnumFields
    .filter((f) => f.isId || f.isUnique)
    .map((f) => `  ${f.name}?: ${fieldTsType(f)}`)
    .join('\n')

  return [
    `export interface ${model.name} {\n${mainFields}\n}`,
    `export interface ${model.name}CreateInput {\n${createFields}\n}`,
    `export interface ${model.name}UpdateInput {\n${updateFields}\n}`,
    `export interface ${model.name}WhereUniqueInput {\n${whereUniqueFields}\n}`,
  ].join('\n\n')
}
```

### `packages/client-generator-rest/src/render-model-client.ts`

Renders the `ModelRestClient` class. URL path is `modelname + 's'` (lowercase, naively pluralized). `Object.values(where)[0]` extracts the identifying value at runtime — type-safe at call-sites since callers must provide at least one key.

```typescript
import type * as DMMF from '@prisma/dmmf'

export function renderModelClient(model: DMMF.Model): string {
  const name = model.name
  const path = `${name.toLowerCase()}s`

  return `export class ${name}RestClient {
  #baseUrl: string

  constructor(baseUrl: string) {
    this.#baseUrl = baseUrl
  }

  async findMany(params?: Record<string, string>): Promise<${name}[]> {
    const query = params ? '?' + new URLSearchParams(params).toString() : ''
    const res = await fetch(\`\${this.#baseUrl}/${path}\${query}\`)
    return res.json() as Promise<${name}[]>
  }

  async findUnique(where: ${name}WhereUniqueInput): Promise<${name} | null> {
    const id = Object.values(where)[0]
    const res = await fetch(\`\${this.#baseUrl}/${path}/\${id}\`)
    if (res.status === 404) return null
    return res.json() as Promise<${name}>
  }

  async create(data: ${name}CreateInput): Promise<${name}> {
    const res = await fetch(\`\${this.#baseUrl}/${path}\`, {
      method: 'POST',
      headers: { 'Content-Type': 'application/json' },
      body: JSON.stringify(data),
    })
    return res.json() as Promise<${name}>
  }

  async update(where: ${name}WhereUniqueInput, data: ${name}UpdateInput): Promise<${name}> {
    const id = Object.values(where)[0]
    const res = await fetch(\`\${this.#baseUrl}/${path}/\${id}\`, {
      method: 'PUT',
      headers: { 'Content-Type': 'application/json' },
      body: JSON.stringify(data),
    })
    return res.json() as Promise<${name}>
  }

  async delete(where: ${name}WhereUniqueInput): Promise<${name}> {
    const id = Object.values(where)[0]
    const res = await fetch(\`\${this.#baseUrl}/${path}/\${id}\`, { method: 'DELETE' })
    return res.json() as Promise<${name}>
  }
}`
}
```

### `packages/client-generator-rest/src/generate-rest-client.ts`

Orchestrates rendering for all models and enums, assembles the output string, and writes `rest-client.ts` to the output directory.

```typescript
import type * as DMMF from '@prisma/dmmf'
import { mkdir, writeFile } from 'node:fs/promises'
import path from 'node:path'

import { renderModelClient } from './render-model-client'
import { renderEnum, renderModelTypes } from './render-model-types'

export async function generateRestClient(dmmf: DMMF.Document, outputDir: string): Promise<void> {
  const { models, enums } = dmmf.datamodel

  const modelProperties = models
    .map((model) => {
      const prop = `${model.name.charAt(0).toLowerCase()}${model.name.slice(1)}`
      return `  readonly ${prop} = new ${model.name}RestClient(this.#baseUrl)`
    })
    .join('\n')

  const restClientClass = `export class RestClient {
  #baseUrl: string

  constructor(baseUrl: string) {
    this.#baseUrl = baseUrl
  }

${modelProperties}
}`

  const sections = [
    '// This file was generated by @prisma/client-generator-rest. Do not edit.',
    ...enums.map(renderEnum),
    ...models.map(renderModelTypes),
    ...models.map(renderModelClient),
    restClientClass,
  ]

  await mkdir(outputDir, { recursive: true })
  await writeFile(path.join(outputDir, 'rest-client.ts'), sections.join('\n\n') + '\n', 'utf-8')
}
```

### `packages/client-generator-rest/src/generator.ts`

```typescript
import { Generator, GeneratorConfig, GeneratorManifest, GeneratorOptions } from '@prisma/generator'
import { parseEnvValue } from '@prisma/internals'

import { version } from '../package.json'
import { generateRestClient } from './generate-rest-client'

export class RestClientGenerator implements Generator {
  readonly name = 'prisma-client-rest'

  getManifest(_config: GeneratorConfig): Promise<GeneratorManifest> {
    return Promise.resolve({
      prettyName: 'Prisma REST Client',
      defaultOutput: 'generated/rest-client',
      version,
      requiresEngines: [],
    })
  }

  async generate(options: GeneratorOptions): Promise<void> {
    const outputDir = parseEnvValue(options.generator.output!)
    await generateRestClient(options.dmmf, outputDir)
  }
}
```

### `packages/client-generator-rest/src/index.ts`

```typescript
export { generateRestClient } from './generate-rest-client'
export { RestClientGenerator } from './generator'
```

---

## Files to Modify

### 1. `packages/client-generator-registry/package.json`

Add `@prisma/client-generator-rest` to `dependencies` (alphabetical, after `client-generator-js`):

```json
"dependencies": {
  "@prisma/client-generator-js": "workspace:*",
  "@prisma/client-generator-rest": "workspace:*",
  "@prisma/client-generator-ts": "workspace:*",
  "@prisma/generator": "workspace:*",
  "@prisma/internals": "workspace:*"
}
```

### 2. `packages/client-generator-registry/src/default.ts`

Add import and registration at the end (preserves existing ordering):

```typescript
import { PrismaClientJsGenerator } from '@prisma/client-generator-js'
import { RestClientGenerator } from '@prisma/client-generator-rest'
import { PrismaClientTsGenerator } from '@prisma/client-generator-ts'

import { GeneratorRegistry } from './registry'

export const defaultRegistry = new GeneratorRegistry()

defaultRegistry.add(new PrismaClientJsGenerator())

const tsGenerator = new PrismaClientTsGenerator()
defaultRegistry.add(tsGenerator)
defaultRegistry.addAliased('prisma-client', tsGenerator)

defaultRegistry.add(new RestClientGenerator())
```

### 3. `packages/client-generator-registry/src/default.test.ts`

Update the snapshot to include the new generator name:

```typescript
// old:
expect(Object.keys(generators)).toEqual(['prisma-client-js', 'prisma-client-ts', 'prisma-client'])
// new:
expect(Object.keys(generators)).toEqual(['prisma-client-js', 'prisma-client-ts', 'prisma-client', 'prisma-client-rest'])
```

### 4. `tsconfig.build.bundle.json`

Add path mapping entry after `@prisma/client-generator-registry` (alphabetical):

```json
"@prisma/client-generator-rest": ["./packages/client-generator-rest/src"],
```

### 5. `.github/workflows/test-template.yml`

Add a test step in the `client-packages` job after `client-generator-ts` (line ~588) and before `client-generator-registry`:

```yaml
      - run: pnpm run test
        name: 'client-generator-rest'
        working-directory: packages/client-generator-rest
```

---

## Dependency Graph

```
@prisma/dmmf ──┐
@prisma/generator ──┤──► @prisma/client-generator-rest ──► @prisma/client-generator-registry
@prisma/internals ──┘
```

Turborepo infers build order from the `"workspace:*"` declarations; no changes to `turbo.json` are needed. The package is auto-discovered via the `packages/*` glob in `pnpm-workspace.yaml`.

---

## Verification

1. **Install**: `pnpm install` — confirm workspace resolves the new package.
2. **Build new package**: `pnpm --filter @prisma/client-generator-rest build` — check `dist/index.js` and `dist/index.mjs` are produced.
3. **Build registry**: `pnpm --filter @prisma/client-generator-registry build` — succeeds with the new dep.
4. **Unit test registry**: `pnpm --filter @prisma/client-generator-registry test` — `default.test.ts` passes with the updated key list.
5. **Type check**: `pnpm tsc -p tsconfig.build.bundle.json --noEmit` — new path mapping resolves cleanly; `default.ts` type-checks.
6. **End-to-end smoke test**: With a minimal schema containing a `User` model, run `prisma generate` (with the `rest` generator configured). Confirm `rest-client.ts` is written and contains `export enum` (if enums present), `export interface User`, `export interface UserCreateInput`, `export interface UserUpdateInput`, `export interface UserWhereUniqueInput`, `export class UserRestClient`, and `export class RestClient`.

```typescript
  if (field.kind === 'scalar') return scalarToTsType(field.type)
  if (field.kind === 'enum') return field.type
  // object (relation): typed as the related model, always nullable
  return `${field.type} | null`
}

export function renderEnum(e: DMMF.DatamodelEnum): string {
  const members = e.values.map((v) => `  ${v.name} = '${v.name}',`).join('\n')
  return `export enum ${e.name} {\n${members}\n}`
}

export function renderModelTypes(model: DMMF.Model): string {
  const scalarAndEnumFields = model.fields.filter((f) => f.kind === 'scalar' || f.kind === 'enum')
  const relationFields = model.fields.filter((f) => f.kind === 'object')

  const mainFields = [
    ...scalarAndEnumFields.map((f) => `  ${f.name}${f.isRequired ? '' : '?'}: ${fieldTsType(f)}`),
    ...relationFields.map((f) => `  ${f.name}?: ${f.type} | null`),
  ].join('\n')

  // Required when the field is non-nullable and carries no default (e.g. no autoincrement, no @default)
  const createFields = scalarAndEnumFields
    .map((f) => `  ${f.name}${f.isRequired && !f.hasDefaultValue ? '' : '?'}: ${fieldTsType(f)}`)
    .join('\n')

  const updateFields = scalarAndEnumFields.map((f) => `  ${f.name}?: ${fieldTsType(f)}`).join('\n')

  const whereUniqueFields = scalarAndEnumFields
    .filter((f) => f.isId || f.isUnique)
    .map((f) => `  ${f.name}?: ${fieldTsType(f)}`)
    .join('\n')

  return [
    `export interface ${model.name} {\n${mainFields}\n}`,
    `export interface ${model.name}CreateInput {\n${createFields}\n}`,
    `export interface ${model.name}UpdateInput {\n${updateFields}\n}`,
    `export interface ${model.name}WhereUniqueInput {\n${whereUniqueFields}\n}`,
  ].join('\n\n')
}
```

### `packages/client-generator-rest/src/render-model-client.ts`

Renders the `ModelRestClient` class. URL path is `modelname + 's'` (lowercase, naively pluralized). `Object.values(where)[0]` extracts the identifying value at runtime — type-safe at call-sites since callers must provide at least one key.

```typescript
import type * as DMMF from '@prisma/dmmf'

export function renderModelClient(model: DMMF.Model): string {
  const name = model.name
  const path = `${name.toLowerCase()}s`

  return `export class ${name}RestClient {
  #baseUrl: string

  constructor(baseUrl: string) {
    this.#baseUrl = baseUrl
  }

  async findMany(params?: Record<string, string>): Promise<${name}[]> {
    const query = params ? '?' + new URLSearchParams(params).toString() : ''
    const res = await fetch(\`\${this.#baseUrl}/${path}\${query}\`)
    return res.json() as Promise<${name}[]>
  }

  async findUnique(where: ${name}WhereUniqueInput): Promise<${name} | null> {
    const id = Object.values(where)[0]
    const res = await fetch(\`\${this.#baseUrl}/${path}/\${id}\`)
    if (res.status === 404) return null
    return res.json() as Promise<${name}>
  }

  async create(data: ${name}CreateInput): Promise<${name}> {
    const res = await fetch(\`\${this.#baseUrl}/${path}\`, {
      method: 'POST',
      headers: { 'Content-Type': 'application/json' },
      body: JSON.stringify(data),
    })
    return res.json() as Promise<${name}>
  }

  async update(where: ${name}WhereUniqueInput, data: ${name}UpdateInput): Promise<${name}> {
    const id = Object.values(where)[0]
    const res = await fetch(\`\${this.#baseUrl}/${path}/\${id}\`, {
      method: 'PUT',
      headers: { 'Content-Type': 'application/json' },
      body: JSON.stringify(data),
    })
    return res.json() as Promise<${name}>
  }

  async delete(where: ${name}WhereUniqueInput): Promise<${name}> {
    const id = Object.values(where)[0]
    const res = await fetch(\`\${this.#baseUrl}/${path}/\${id}\`, { method: 'DELETE' })
    return res.json() as Promise<${name}>
  }
}`
}
```

### `packages/client-generator-rest/src/generate-rest-client.ts`

Orchestrates rendering for all models and enums, assembles the output string, and writes `rest-client.ts` to the output directory.

```typescript
import type * as DMMF from '@prisma/dmmf'
import { mkdir, writeFile } from 'node:fs/promises'
import path from 'node:path'

import { renderModelClient } from './render-model-client'
import { renderEnum, renderModelTypes } from './render-model-types'

export async function generateRestClient(dmmf: DMMF.Document, outputDir: string): Promise<void> {
  const { models, enums } = dmmf.datamodel

  const modelProperties = models
    .map((model) => {
      const prop = `${model.name.charAt(0).toLowerCase()}${model.name.slice(1)}`
      return `  readonly ${prop} = new ${model.name}RestClient(this.#baseUrl)`
    })
    .join('\n')

  const restClientClass = `export class RestClient {
  #baseUrl: string

  constructor(baseUrl: string) {
    this.#baseUrl = baseUrl
  }

${modelProperties}
}`

  const sections = [
    '// This file was generated by @prisma/client-generator-rest. Do not edit.',
    ...enums.map(renderEnum),
    ...models.map(renderModelTypes),
    ...models.map(renderModelClient),
    restClientClass,
  ]

  await mkdir(outputDir, { recursive: true })
  await writeFile(path.join(outputDir, 'rest-client.ts'), sections.join('\n\n') + '\n', 'utf-8')
}
```

### `packages/client-generator-rest/src/generator.ts`

```typescript
import { Generator, GeneratorConfig, GeneratorManifest, GeneratorOptions } from '@prisma/generator'
import { parseEnvValue } from '@prisma/internals'

import { version } from '../package.json'
import { generateRestClient } from './generate-rest-client'

export class RestClientGenerator implements Generator {
  readonly name = 'prisma-client-rest'

  getManifest(_config: GeneratorConfig): Promise<GeneratorManifest> {
    return Promise.resolve({
      prettyName: 'Prisma REST Client',
      defaultOutput: 'generated/rest-client',
      version,
      requiresEngines: [],
    })
  }

  async generate(options: GeneratorOptions): Promise<void> {
    const outputDir = parseEnvValue(options.generator.output!)
    await generateRestClient(options.dmmf, outputDir)
  }
}
```


# Plan: Add `@prisma/client-generator-rest` — Typed REST API Client Generator

## Context

The task is to add a new Prisma generator package that reads the DMMF (Data Model Meta Format) produced by `prisma generate` and writes a typed TypeScript REST API client into the configured output directory. For every Prisma model it emits: a main interface, three input-type interfaces (Create/Update/WhereUnique), and a `ModelRestClient` class with `findMany`, `findUnique`, `create`, `update`, `delete` methods that call a backing REST API via native `fetch`. All models are aggregated into a single `RestClient` entry-point class. Enums are emitted as TypeScript `enum` declarations.

The new package follows the exact same layout as `@prisma/client-generator-js` (bundled with `adapterConfig`, implements the `Generator` interface from `@prisma/generator`) and is registered in `@prisma/client-generator-registry` so the CLI can resolve it by its provider name `"prisma-client-rest"`.

---

## Files to Create

```json
  "module": "dist/index.mjs",
  "types": "dist/index.d.ts",
  "exports": {
    ".": {
      "require": {
        "types": "./dist/index.d.ts",
        "default": "./dist/index.js"
      },
      "import": {
        "types": "./dist/index.d.mts",
        "default": "./dist/index.mjs"
      }
    }
  },
  "repository": {
    "type": "git",
    "url": "https://github.com/prisma/prisma.git",

  },

    "@prisma/generator": "workspace:*",
    "@prisma/internals": "workspace:*"

    "dev": "DEV=true tsx helpers/build.ts",
```
